title: So funktioniert die Wärme-Einbettung eines Tappex® Gewindeeinsatzes
description:
In warmem Kunststoff feste Verbindungen schaffen:

Bei thermoplastischen Werkstoffen ist es möglich, dauerhaften Halt durch Wärmeverfahren zu erreichen. Tappex® bietet für diesen Fall Profi-Elemente.

Eine Verbindung bei Kunststoffen kann durch Einpressen, Ultraschall-Einbettung oder – wie im Video gezeigt – durch lokales Schmelzen des Kunststoffes geschehen. Tappex® Gewindeeinsätze bieten dafür ein Innengewinde in Kombination mit einem Zackenverankerungsprofil, das patentiert wurde.

Für alle Kunststoff-verarbeitenden Industrien ist diese effiziente und vielfältig einsetzbare Technik relevant. 

Weitere Informationen finden Sie unter: 
http://www.kvt-fastening.de/produkte/marken
http://www.tappex.co.uk

title: Food grade first for Kingston
description:
With concern increasing about the mineral oil contamination of food products by packaging, the Kingston Carton Company is the first UK carton maker to implement mineral oil free manufacturing using BOBST’s food-grade lubricant transformation programme.

“It wasn’t the cost of replacing the clients’ packaging that worried us. It was the cost of replacing the food that goes in it,” says Arend Buitendam, Managing Director of The Kingston Carton Company, explaining why the company has implemented a BOBST programme to migrate its conversion equipment from lubricants based on mineral oils to solely food grade synthetic lubricants.

Read more: http://media.bobst.com/en/releases/detail/article/1457101710-food-grade-first-for-kingston

title: ABB LB Pipe Svia
description:
LB Pipe & Coupling Products, LLC (lbpipeandcouplingproducts.com)  has accelerated its growth by installing two robotic machine tending systems from SVIA LLC, US (SVIA.com). Founded in 2010,  LB Pipe of Magnolia, Texas manufactures high-quality precision couplings for the water well, oil country industry and American Petroleum Institute (API)-certified markets. 

In moving to user friendly robotic automation, LB Pipe has been able to increase production and product quality, while providing a safer environment and increased responsibilities for its employees.

SVIA LLC, US specializes in equipping industrial robots with vision enabled functionality for machine tool tending systems in manufacturing for such applications as CNC lathes, vertical machine centers, horizontal machine centers and grinders.  

Each system features an ABB IRB 6700 industrial robot, ABB’s seventh generation of industry-leading, large industrial robots,  delivering enhanced speed, payload, accuracy and a 20% lower total cost of ownership than comparable robot models (ABB.com/robotics).

title: ABB Robotics - Painting and sealing innovation with Volvo
description:
The new Volvo S60L that went on sale in December 2013 in Beijing provides drivers with a quieter, cleaner, safer and more comfortable driving environment, thanks in part to ABB Robotics.

The S60Lis an important car for Volvo, having achieved many “firsts” along the way. For instance, it’s the very first model to be mass-produced at the company’s factory in Chengdu, China—which happens to be the first such factory to be located outside Europe since the Swedish company was acquired by Zhejiang Geely Holding Group in 2010. It is also the first process-validated car produced in China by Volvo using an ABB Robotics solution.

As China’s first automobile plant to use an environmentally-friendly, water-based soundproofing and sealing material provided by ABB, Volvo is also exploring new ways to push the barrier for improving the quality of the vehicle interior environment. To implement the use of this waterborne material—developed over a six-month period—required building a fully automated gluing station where robots spray the material onto the cars, thereby sealing, soundproofing and waterproofing them. 

Thirty-three different ABB robots are now hard at work in the Volvo Chengdu plant. Among them, two robots specialize in NVH applications, two in sprayable sound dampening applications, four in exterior-primer painting, eight in exterior-basecoat painting, four in exterior clearcoat painting, four painting robots, four door openers with one trunk opener in a clearcoat interior station, and four FeatherDuster robots at the pre-cleaning station.

title: ABB Robotics - Engineering with LEGO can transform the future
description:
It took eight months to build using only spare time, but a fully programmable, near life-size ABB robot made out of LEGO bricks is a spectacular thing to behold. 

Yes, in this age of limited attention spans and virality-driven media perhaps it's cool enough that a near life-size industrial robot has been recreated in LEGO bricks, but there's so much more behind this story that it would be sad if it stopped there.

In particular, it's clear that there is a mismatch between the skills that currently exist in the workforce and the skills we need for the robotics and tech heavy world of the future. What better way to get kids and young adults interested in science, technology, engineering and math (STEM) than through programmable LEGO?

Come along and join us as we explore this story.

title: ABB Robotics - Remote Service and the Internet of Things
description:
Remote Service and the Internet of Things can change the way you interact with your robots and increase uptime whilst reducing worry.

By connecting our deep experience in customer service with the power of the Internet, we have made it a reality for our customers to change from a “break then fix” conventional approach, into a predictive, proactive, and immediate support mindset. This is accomplished by merging our Remote Service platform with the burgeoning explosion of the Internet of Things.

ABB’s Remote Service product monitors the health of your robots 24 hours a day and 7 days per week year round through a wireless connection to the Internet. 

“Before the days of the Internet of Things, customers would maintain their robots according to a certain schedule,” says Rene Nispeling, ABB Robotics Product Manager for Customer Service, “but not every robot is used in the same way. 

Thanks to the Internet of Things we can see how a robot is used via a ‘black box’ we can install in our robots that provides us with data over the Internet. Things such as torques, loads, and usage hours are communicated in real time and allow us to adjust maintenance schedules to each individual robot and predict maintenance needs. That reduces the cost to a customer and increases the lifetime of that robot.”

title: Magnetic Strayfield Immunity (Japanese) / 漂遊磁場耐性
description:
モーターが作動していたり、高電圧の電気系統など、強い磁力を含む環境で使用するシステムを設計するのは、エンジニアにとっては頭を悩ませるところです。
システムの信頼性と安全性を確保するには、漂遊磁場耐性が決定的に重要になってきます。amsはビルトイン耐性を持つ、特許を取得した独自の差動原理を開発し、どんな磁力からも完璧に保護しながら安全かつ信頼できる動作を提供することで、この問題に取り組んできました。不要な（漂遊する）磁場への耐性をそなえたamsのポジションセンサは、既存の、そして将来登場する多様な車両用および産業用アプリケーションに信頼性と安全性を提供します。

title: ABB Robotics - Production Screen - HMIs made easy
description:
ABB Production Screen brings smart phone logic to the ABB robot HMI device, the FlexPendant. The new software package uses graphical objects, known as widgets, to perform basic robot functions. Widgets can be created using either ABB ScreenMaker or third-party software such as Microsoft Visual Studio. 
The software's on-screen customization and user-friendly navigation are designed for use with a variety of applications such as arc welding, spot welding and dispensing. Widgets can be programmed to perform information, control, monitoring and notification functions. Users simply choose which widgets they need as define what they should look like. Ready-made widgets and apps also can be shared and downloaded from ABB's application library RobotApps

"Thanks to smart phones we've learned to take simple graphic interface configuration for granted," says ABB Product Manager Per Loewgren. "Production Screen is an easy and familiar way to customize a FlexPendant just like a smart phone."

title: ABB Robotics - Applying polyester resin to granite slabs
description:
In marble polishing, the use of polyester resin to fill and reinforce the slabs has been an accepted solution for over 50 years. Resin fillers are mainly used for aesthetic effects and to avoid open holes in the surface of the material.

The more fragile marbles have also a reinforced backing support, usually in a form of a fiberglass mesh. In this process, the main material used with the net is a polyester resin. 

Now robot itegrator T&D Robotics in Italy have fully automated this process using ABB robots. Benefits include reduced working area, lower energy costs & improved safety levels.

title: ABB Robotics - Technology Days 2013 Flyover
description:
ABB Robotics hosted over 1,000 people during its annual Technology Day event May 15, 2013, at its North American headquarters and training center in Auburn Hills, Michigan.  The attendees included current and potential customers, value providers, affiliated technology equipment providers, members of the media, and for the late afternoon Open House, a fascinated crowd of children, friends and robot enthusiasts of all ages. The 55,000 sq ft exhibit floor included over 40 live robot related demonstrations from ABB and over a dozen peripheral equipment providers.  The program also included 45 small-group, topic-specific seminars on many of the same technologies featured on the demo floor.  

ABB Robotics product experts from all over the world were on hand to discuss emerging technologies in applications including arc and laser welding, picking, packing and palletizing, press automation, painting, advanced assembly applications, machine tending, and the full range of ABB's service and training programs.

"The last few years have been tremendous for the industrial robot community, and the range of new technologies and the enthusiasm of the Technology Days crowd bode very well for the future," said Michael Mahfet, the vice president and general manager of ABB Robotics, North America. "We had more robots on the floor and a more diverse group of industrial segment attendees than ever before."

title: ABB Robotics - Integrated Vision
description: ABB Integrated Vision is a powerful smart camera system that makes vision-guided robotics applications faster and easier to deploy than ever before. Using 2D vision guidance, manufacturers have the ability to: track products more accurately, improve supply chain management, improve quality, troubleshoot challenging lines and processes and significantly expand their use of robotic automation. Taken together, the advantages of vision-guided robotics can significantly improve a company's bottom line, saving time, money, and resources.

title: LitPol Link – Securing power supply in the Baltic Region
description:
LitPol, a 500-megawatt, “back-to-back” high-voltage direct current (HVDC) link, interconnects the power grids of Lithuania and Poland. This interconnection enables electricity to be traded between the two countries, strengthening and securing the power supply in the whole region.

Delivered by ABB, the leading power and automation group, this project contributes to the EU’s Baltic Ring power initiative. The initiative’s goal is to create a cross-national grid between nine countries around the Baltic Sea - Lithuania, Poland, Germany, Denmark, Norway, Sweden, Finland, Estonia and Latvia. With the completion of the LitPol link, all these states are now joined, just one of 16 highly sophisticated HVDC projects ABB has been involved in in this region.
Reference web page: http://new.abb.com/systems/hvdc/references/litpol-link

title: ABB to power “superhighway” bringing electricity to millions in India
description:
The 800-kilovolt North-East Agra ultrahigh-voltage direct current (UHVDC) link will transmit clean hydroelectric power, equivalent to the generation of eight large power plants, from India's northeast region to the city of Agra, a distance of 1,728 kilometers. UHVDC transmission is a development of HVDC, a preferred solution to transmit large amounts of power over long distances to high consumption centers with minimum losses. The North-East Agra project, which will have a record converter capacity, is ABB’s fifth HVDC transmission link in India. 
http://new.abb.com/systems/hvdc/references/north-east-agra

title: ABB Robotics - Folding Metal Sheets (Press Brake Tending)
description: Vik Industrier of Norway make large metal facade panels for buildings. They wanted to increase flexibility in their pressing & forming operations and have installed a URSVIKEN folding machine controlled by an ABB robot. The metal sheets are up to 4m long and 1m wide & required two operators to lift them. Now the robot safely feeds the machine & also controls the press as a remote 7th axis. This means the total system can now be programmed & simulated off line using ABB's RobotStudio software. To further utilize the robot's flexibility it is equiped with a tool changer that allows it to place pre-prepared tooling into the forming machine thereby reducing downtime.

title: ABB Robotics - new mid-range robot family - IRB 2600
description:
ABB further extends its mid-range robot family with the introduction of the new IRB 2600 range of robots. The IRB 2600 family contains 3 models with long reach and 12 to 20 kg handling capacity, all of which are designed to increase productivity in machine tending, material handling, arc welding and other process applications. 

These new robots are light, fast, accurate and compact and have many mounting possibilities, including floor, ceiling, shelf, wall & tilted. This ensures that cells can be designed to optimize valuable manufacturing floor space.

The reduced arm weight, combined with ABB's patented second generation QuickMove & TrueMove motion control software, ensures very fast & accurate movements and results in cycle times being reduced by up to 25%.
For more information (and more movies) vist the IRB 2600 product page:
http://www.abb.com/product/seitp327/b0f49525bc49b32ac12576a200431318.aspx?country=US

title: Alltag mit Fingergeschwüren
description:
Alltag einer Patientin mit Fingergeschwüren

„Nichts geht mehr leicht von der Hand"

Stellen sie sich vor, sie können ihre Hände nicht mehr gebrauchen, weil sie stark schmerzende Wunden haben, die schlecht heilen. Schon eine Bluse zuzuknöpfen wird zur Herausforderung und den Käse zum Frühstück aus der Plastikverpackung zu entnehmen, ist schier unmöglich. Die meisten Patienten können nicht mehr arbeiten, denn auch körperlich leichte Tätigkeiten, z.B. im Büro, scheitern bereits am Tippen auf dem Computer. Und auch im sozialen Miteinander sind die Veränderungen an den Händen ein Hemmnis. So müssen die Betroffenen immer wieder mit dem Unverständnis für ihre seltene Erkrankung und mit der Abneigung ihrer Mitmenschen zurechtkommen. Ein Video zeigt jetzt diese Erfahrungen: Aus Patientensicht und ohne viele Worte auskommend, zeigt der Film reale Situationen und verschont den Zuschauer nicht vor dem „wahren Gesicht" der Erkrankung. 

Alles fängt mit einem Weißwerden der Finger, dem so genannten Raynaud-Phänomen, an. Später können die äußerst schmerzhaften Fingergeschwüre (digitale Ulzerationen) hinzukommen. Die digitalen Ulzerationen sind eine Begleiterscheinung der seltenen Autoimmunerkrankung systemische Sklerose, bei der es zu Verhärtungen und Ver-steifungen des Bindegewebes kommt. Das Fatale daran: Bindegewebe ist ein überall im Körper vorkommender Baustein, so dass es neben Hautverhärtungen auch zu Schädigungen an allen Organen und Blutgefäßen kommen kann. Sichtbare Zeichen der Erkrankung sind die typischen Veränderungen im Gesicht: Der Gesichtsausdruck wirkt maskenhaft und der Mund lässt sich nicht mehr weit öffnen. An den Händen sind die beschriebenen Geschwüre ein deutliches Zeichen für die Erkrankung. 

Aber nicht jedem Weißwerden der Finger liegt zwangsläufig diese schwere Erkrankung zu Grunde. Es gibt auch eine harmlose Form des Raynaud-Syndroms. Um sicher zu erkennen, um welche Form es sich handelt, sollten Betroffene einen Rheumatologen oder einen Hautarzt, die Experten für diese Erkrankung, aufsuchen. Denn digitale Ulzerationen können heute mit Medikamenten weitgehend verhindert werden. 

Informationen unter:
www.sklerodermie-info.de
www.scleroliga.de
www.sklerodermie-selbsthilfe.de
www.actelion.de

title: ABB’s Motor Drive 1.4 – making switchgear intelligent
description:
Motor drive 1.4 is a digitally controlled servomotor that drives the contacts of a high-voltage circuit-breaker with the highest precision. The drive is equipped with an electronic local control cabinet which enables the high-voltage switching bay to be operated digitally with a Human Machine Interface. Motor Drive 1.4 provides embedded self-monitoring and diagnostics for switchgear parameters. Monitoring, event log download and operations are available locally and remotely, securing effective preventive maintenance and remote service.

title: ABB Robotics - Packing Cheese
description:
At dairy giant Arla Foods' factory in Sweden the repetitive work of packing cheese into boxes was taking its toll on the workforce. Placing blocks of cheese into a carton at the rate of nearly one per second caused high rates of repetitive strain injuries. The solution was to automate with four packing cells from Graniten, each built around an IRB 120 robot from ABB. Thomas Johansson project manager at Arla FoodsJohansson says he is pleased with
Graniten's solution and adds that the workers who are now monitoring the robots rather than packing cheese by hand welcome the fact that they are no longer exposed to potential injury. He says, "The robots can't do everything that a person can do on this workstation, but they can certainly do 98--99 percent of the job. "

title: ABB Mining User Conference 2013 - Wrap-up Video

title: Multi Medical Alarm Module from Ascom Wireless Solutions
description:
This films shows how the Ascom Multi Medical Alarm Module (MMA) functions, and it illustrates the many benefits it implies for the user.

The MMA from Ascom Wireless Solutions is a medical device that extends alarm notification for patient medical devices like infusion pumps to wireless devices. The MMA supports up to four medical device interfaces for non-critical care patient rooms, and alarms are triggered by contact closure. When a medical device alarm is triggered, an alarm message is sent to caregiver's wireless device. The MMA relies on a teleCARE IP system to monitor and deliver medical device alarm notifications, hence a teleCARE IP system is required for MMA deployment. 

The MMA is always connected to either a bedside module, a medical rail socket and/or a pull cord (for Nordic markets). A "green light" indicator assures that the connected device(s) is properly connected. 

The MMA is a medical device and CE marked accordantly. It is designed to comply with Medical Device Directive (MDD 93/42/EEC) and is approved for EU and EFTA countries only.

Vistit: http://www.ascom.com/ws

title: How to program the AS5600
description:
This video illustrates how to program and design with the AS5600, the magnetic rotary position sensor, delivered by ams.

The AS5600 is a new magnetic position sensor with analog, PWM-encoded or I2C output. The high reliability, durability, simple angular excursion programming and automatic low power modes make this solution ideally suited for contactless potentiometers.

In summary, the AS5600 offers three programming options:
Option A: Programming start and stop position over I2C
Option B: Programming in 3-wire mode
Option C: Programming a angular range over I2C
With the AS5600 it is easier to set-up a highly reliable and robust contactless potentiometer.

title: AFG /  Piatti Taxi Tv-Spot (französisch)
ABB Robotics - Flexible Machine Tool Tending with Husqvarna
description:
The new FlexMT™ from ABB Robotics a turnkey solution for vision guided machine tending. It guarantees constant and predictable output from the machine tool, and facilitates higher machine tool utilization, up to an amazing 90 percent, compared with traditional machine tools which are closer to 50 percent. 

Husqvarna, the well known global outdoor power tool producer, has found that the FlexMT has significantly improved their productivity as well as increased their efficiency by between 20 and 25 percent.

With the aid of vision-guided robotics, our solution loads and unloads traditional machine tools including horizontal and vertical lathes and machining centers as well as 5-axis machines and grinders. Available in two variants, FlexMT 20 (20kg/1.65m reach) and the FlexMT 60 (60kg, 2.05m reach), both come complete with a robot controller inside its fully integrated controller cabinet.

title: ABB Robotics - Machine Tending & Assembly
description: ABB are always happy to practice what they preach. In this movie a IRB 1600 industrial robot assembles light switches in the ABB Elektro-Praga facility in Jablonec in the Czech Republic. The robot extracts 4 molded light switch covers from an injection molding machine. The covers are then placed on a cooling station. During the molding cycle (29 secs) the robot undertakes a secondary task - to assemble the cool light switch covers to their base. Those bases are fed through a bowlfeeder on an indexing conveyor which stops in front of the robot to enable the assembly. Once assembled, the light switches move to a flow wrapper and end up packed in a container ready for shipment.

title: Fairbanks Alaska - World’s most powerful battery energy storage
description:
The battery energy storage system (BESS) for Golden Valley Electric Association (GVEA) has helped this Alaskan utility to reduce power outages due to grid faults by 90% over the past five years. In 2013, the BESS responded to 60 generation and transmission outage events, preventing a total of 310,492 consumer outages – crucial with winter temperatures as low as -50°C. The BESS gives GVEA ample time to start up local generation in case of power line failures. It acts as spinning reserve and provides VAR support for power system stabilization. The BESS won a Guinness World Records entry as the world’s most powerful battery storage by providing 46 megawatts (MW) of power for five minutes during a test in 2003.

title: The Art and Science of Prediction: From Big Data to Big Insights
description:
For as long as there have been financial markets, analysts have constructed models in an attempt to predict future price movements. For the most part, their method has centered on analyzing the past in the hope of divining the future.

But what other tools are there to predict future behavior? What can be learned from seemingly unrelated fields of knowledge, such as epidemiology, entomology (more specifically the life of bees), and psychology? What do these new tools have in common? And how to incorporate them into existing models?

At Credit Suisse’s Thought Leader Forum in June, we asked these questions and more to an array of experts in the financial industry and beyond. Their answers? Watch the video to find out how we continue to refine the art and science of prediction.

title: ABB's offering for Nedalco's ethanol production plant in Manchester
description: Nedalco is a leading European producer of natural ethanol (ethyl alcohol), with activities in about 20 countries worldwide and four production sites in Europe. Their plant in Manchester takes waste wheat sugars from a neighbouring food manufacturing plant and turns them into 40 million litres per annum of top quality alcohol, used in beverage, fuel and as an additive in perfume, cosmetics, pharmaceutical products and printing inks. By-products of the process are used in the production of animal feeds for cattle and pigs. ABB helped with commissioning the Extended Automation System 800xA control system and provides round-the-clock technical support in the form of telephone access to ABB automation experts. As well as supplying preventive maintenance services, ABB will also help with any future modifications or upgrades to improve the system. Modern control systems are very complex. To ensure maximum reliability and availability, professional support is required -- downtime for such plants costs huge amounts of money.

With Symphony® Plus automation system, all essential data are collected to grant optimum control of plant assets. It also monitors components and analyzes alarms, providing precise diagnostics and KPI reports that reduce or eliminate downtime for maximum wind plant performance and return on investment. title: Copper mining at its most efficient
description: At Sweden largest copper mine, Boliden Aitik, it takes 1000 kgs of earth to obtain three kgs of copper. The mine, located near Gällivare inside the Arctic Circle, is only profitable because its processes are highly automated. Mined ore is transported by a conveyor belt seven kilometers long to a processing plant where giant mills grind it into sand from which the copper can be extracted. The entire site process -- including the concentrator plant, conveyor systems and pumping stations -- is controlled by ABB's Extended Automation System 800xA. ABB's scope of supply also included a gas-insulated substation, a harmonics and power factor correction filter system, low-voltage switchgear as well as maintenance and service.

title: Masterbatches in Mosquito Nets
Electronic Pressure Independent Valve
description:
http://www.belimo.us/americas/zonetight.html

The ePIV is a two-way valve which is unaffected by pressure variations in a system. The ePIV directly measures fl ow by combining a magnetic fl ow meter and a 2-way control valve. The actuator has a powerful algorithm that modulates the control valve to maintain the flow setpoint set by the controller. The fl ow reading is reported back to the controller using a standard
signal, and this value can be used by the Building Automation System to perform advanced control and energy strategies. The ePIV is a valve designed specifi cally for HVAC applications.

title: So regelt das SFC KOENIG CHECK VALVE® die Strömungsrichtung
description:
Das professionelle Dichtungs- und Flusssteuersystem für Fluide

Das SFC KOENIG CHECK VALVE® ist eine installationsfreundliche, zuverlässige Lösung zur Steuerung der Strömungsrichtung in Fluidsystemen bis zu einem maximalen Betriebs-/Differenzdruck von 300 bar. Das professionelle Dichtungs- und Flusssteuersystem wird wie die SFC KOENIG EXPANDER® in eine vorhandene Bohrung eingesetzt und der integrierte Pin eingepresst. Die Hülse dehnt sich aus, verankert sich fest im Basismaterial und bietet eine dauerhafte, metallisch dichtende Verbindung.

Das SFC KOENIG CHECK VALVE® wird überall dort eingesetzt, wo es auf die Druckerhaltung und Verhinderung von Rückströmungen und Flüssigkeitsaustritten sowie auf die schnelle und präzise Steuerung von Strömungsrichtungen ankommt – zum Beispiel als Rückflusssperre für Pumpen und Motoren, zur Druckerhaltung in Hydraulikspeichern, zur Steuerung des Flüssigkeitsdrucks in Kreisläufen oder zur Aufrechterhaltung des Ansaugdrucks von Pumpen. Es werden verschiedene Ausführungen sowohl für Vorlauf als auch für Rücklauf angeboten.


Weitere Informationen finden Sie unter:
http://www.kvt-fastening.de/produkte/verbindungstechnologie/dichtungstechnologie/
http://www.sfckoenig.com/de/das-koenig-check-valve%C2%AE

title: ABB & Solar Impulse: making smarter energy choices with Smart Grids in Raleigh, NC
description:
Solar Impulse is an ambassador for lower energy consumption and a greener environment with less CO2 emissions. ABB believes that, in the words of CEO Ulrich Spiesshofer, it is possible to run the world without consuming the Earth.
 
To continue growing the economy without harming the environment, we will need to change the way we produce, transmit and consume energy. This calls for “smart grids”, electricity grids that can guarantee a stable, reliable supply from more unpredictable clean sources (solar, wind) and stored energy.
 
ABB provides utility companies with the hardware and software to prepare for this scenario, one which is certainly more complex but essential for the transition to a cleaner world.

title: ABB Robotics - IRB 6700 technical details
description:
The IRB 6700 family of robots is a natural evolution following 40 years of large robot heritage at ABB.

This 7th generation of large ABB robots features a multitude of next generation improvements derived from intimate customer relationships and exhaustive engineering studies. The IRB 6700 is more robust than its predecessor and maintenance has been simplified, making it the highest performing robot for the lowest total cost of ownership in the 150-300 kg class.

Watch the launch movie: https://youtu.be/dJVzoWn2z9U

Features and benefits:

-Increased service intervals and decreased service times
-Longer uptime—mean time between failures 400,000 hours
-Lean ID for cost effectively increasing dress pack lifetimes
-More robust with a rigid structure
-A new generation of motors and compact gearboxes
-Increased speed and shorter cycle times; average 5 percent faster
-Improved accuracy and higher payloads
-Built to operate in the harshest environments—available with Foundry Plus 2 package
-15 percent lower power consumption

title: ABB Robotics - Flexible Machine Tool Tending with Ravema
description:
The new FlexMT™ from ABB Robotics a turnkey solution for vision guided machine tending. It guarantees constant and predictable output from the machine tool, and facilitates higher machine tool utilization, up to an amazing 90 percent, compared with traditional machine tools which are closer to 50 percent. 

Ravema is a distributor of manufacturing solutions and have found that the FlexMT is the ideal automated machine tending solution their customers are demanding, reducing production times and allowing for incredibly short changover times between production runs.

With the aid of vision-guided robotics, our solution loads and unloads traditional machine tools including horizontal and vertical lathes and machining centers as well as 5-axis machines and grinders. Available in two variants, FlexMT 20 (20kg/1.65m reach) and the FlexMT 60 (60kg, 2.05m reach), both come complete with a robot controller inside its fully integrated controller cabinet.

title: Magnetic Stray Field Immunity
description:
Designing systems for use in environment containing powerful sources of magnetism, such as motor drives and high-voltage power transmission lines, creates a taxing problem for engineers.
To ensure system reliability and safety, stray field immunity has become a crucial requirement in the market. ams has addressed this need by developing a unique patented differential principle with built-in immunity providing a complete protection against any magnetic power and guarantees safe and reliable operation. ams’ position sensors with inherent immunity of unwanted ('stray') magnetic fields deliver reliability and safety for a large variety of present and future automotive and industrial applications.

title: 'ABB Review' celebrates 100 years of innovation

Every year over 1000 people from the ABB integrator network attend one of our seminars. This is a tradition dating back 15 years. The events are a fantastic mixture of business and pleasure. Industry updates, product & application training, knowledge exchange, exhibition of everything new & great opportunities to network with peers - they are all part of the successful mix. 

Many who attend liken the events to a "family reunion". Brant von 't Hof from AWL-Techniek comments "You have to be there. You have to meet the people".  

And of course there are parties. Thomas Rosell of Front Automation has the final word "The party is the most important thing"

title: ABB Robotics - Palletizing - new robots, products & solutions
description:
ABB is further enhancing its current palletizing product line by introducing two new robots, three new grippers, and a comprehensive software program to complement its existing palletizing product range. "We are serious about serving this market," says Klas Bengtsson, Product Manager at ABB Robotics. "The new complete range now addresses all integrator and customer needs for palletizing solutions" 

Leading ABB's new palletizing offering is the compact, 110 kg capacity, IRB 460 - the fastest palletizing robot in the world. Capable of up to 2,190 cycles per hour, this 4-axis robot is perfect for high-speed end-of-line palletizing and bag palletizing. 

For high-output full-layer palletizing, ABB is introducing the IRB 760 robot. Boasting an impressive payload capacity of 450 kilograms and a reach of 3.2 meters, this robot features high wrist inertia -- double that of competitors -- that enables it to rotate heavier and larger products faster than any other robot. 

Three FlexGrippers round out the new hardware product offerings, including a Clamp, which comes in two sizes, for handling cases; a heavy-duty Claw for high-speed bag palletizing; and a Vacuum gripper that can handle up to five products at once. 

The most revolutionary aspect of the new palletizing portfolio is ABB's new software, RobotStudio Palletizing PowerPac. This program allows users to configure, simulate and program ABB robots and grippers for palletizing solutions, with little or no robot programming experience required. 

In summarizing the benefits of the new offerings Bengtsson is quite clear "ABB's complete range of palletizing robots can now be combined with FlexGrippers and Palletizing PowerPac to provide a cost-effective, easy to use, one-stop choice for both end users and system integrators." 

Read more:
http://www.abb.com/product/us/9AAC129475.aspx

title: ABB Robotics - Grinding Forged Steel Parts
description: Donhad Pty. Ltd of Perth manufacture fastenings, mainly for mining and oil & gas industries. They were experiencing major problem with incidents, near misses & hazards in their manual grinding operations. So they asked systems integrator Robotics Solutions WA to look at an automated alternative. The newly installed system, featuring an ABB IRB 6660 Industrial Robot with Force Control & Foundry Protection, has eliminated all problems and resulted in a safe, zero incident, environment.

title: ABB Robotics - Packaging Assembly
description:
Vivabox is one of packaging producer BDMO's largest product lines at its factory in Meulebeke, a town in the Flanders, Belgium. When they needed a solution to their automated packaging assembly needs BDMO requested offers from ABB System Integrator Viscon. Viscon's winning offer proposed a 'pick-and-place' system that could handle 1,200 trays an hour. 
A tight fit problem was resolved by using the 6-axis functionality of ABB's IRB 120 robot; it inserts the tray at an angle before pushing it firmly down to the base and onto spots of glue.
The resulting solution now runs 16 hours a day in two shifts. Needless to say, the cost savings have been significant.
Looking ahead, BDMO will use robots to automate other production processes; for example, inserting one finished box inside another in order to reduce shipping costs.

title: Tony's Chocolonely's new cocoa butter tank in Wieze for fully traceable chocolate
description:
The Barry Callebaut Group and Tony’s Chocolonely, the Amsterdam-based chocolate company committed to bringing an end to slavery in the chocolate industry, announced their strategic partnership agreement to produce chocolate from fully traceable sustainable cocoa. 
For this, Barry Callebaut installed a dedicated cocoa butter tank in its factory in Wieze/Belgium to produce cocoa butter from traceable beans sourced from Tony’s Chocolonely’s partner cooperatives in Côte d’Ivoire. 
With the cocoa liquor already being produced from beans from their partner cooperatives in Côte d’Ivoire and Ghana, all cocoa beans in Tony’s Chocolonely’s chocolate will be fully traceable.

title: Turning on the lights in the Åland islands, Finland – HVDC Light black-start demonstration
description:
Linking the Åland islands and mainland Finland, ABB's high-voltage direct current (HVDC) link provides reliable power to the 29,000 residents. ABB’s ‘black-start’ system, a feature of HVDC technology that can support security of power supply, was recently successfully tested on the islands. These tests showed that power restoration after a complete blackout can take just fractions of a second, so fast that the outage isn't even noticed.

title: The birth of HVDC
description:
In 1954 the world’s first high-voltage direct current (HVDC) transmission link was delivered by ABB. A new technology was born and the inventor, Uno Lamm, became known as the “father of HVDC”. 
In the first half of the last century, Uno Lamm led the project to develop mercury-arc valves so that they could transmit high-voltage power over longer distances. 
http://new.abb.com/systems/hvdc

title: E-LEARNING solutions by CREALOGIX
description:
Do you want to train and further educate your employees? Provide them with new knowledge quickly and efficiently? Then we at CREALOGIX have the perfect solution for you!

We base our approach on a digital learning management system that your employees can access at any time: at their own workplaces, at different locations, on the way to work or on business trips, online or offline. This requires learning content that can be compiled and expanded in modules at any time to meet your needs. We provide comprehensive advice and develop on request your specialist learning modules in interdisciplinary teams and with multimedia know-how.

Our authoring tools make it very easy for you to create your own learning modules, as well as tests and exams and you will be able to track your employees' learning progress at any time and issue certificates. 

All this is hosted for you on a secure cloud solution based in Switzerland and, finally, you can also combine your e-learning campaigns with your instructor-led courses and reflect your employees' progress in the system. as Switzerland's market leader, our solutions are all from one source.

CREALOGIX - Your partner for successful e-learning.

title: Cyberex® PowerBuilt™ industrial UPS offers faster and more accurate fault detection
description: The Cyberex® PowerBuilt™ industrial uninterruptible power supply (UPS) from ABB features a proprietary amplitude detection control design resulting in faster, more accurate fault sensing for system reliability and critical load protection. PowerBuilt™ is a true online double conversion industrial UPS designed to support the continuing demand from downstream refining and petrochemical processing, upstream oil and gas processing, power generation, and the growing regulatory and safety needs of today’s industrial complexes. The PowerBuilt™ series UPS is designed to UL 1778 safety and IEC 62040-3 performance standards; and therefore, it can be scaled to meet changing electrical requirements and is adaptable to the most stringent technical specification.

title: ABB at the SPS IPC Drives exhibition in Nurnberg
description:
ABB recently took part in SPS IPC Drives in Nurnberg Germany, one of the world’s largest exhibitions for electric automation technology with some 55,000 visitors. 

ABB had four focus areas that were based on some of its customer’s biggest challenges today: 1) managing the increasing complexity of automation, 2) ensuring efficient engineering, 3) integrating solutions for functional machine and system safety, and 4) improving energy efficiency. In support of the Next Level strategy, there was also a strong focus on attractive growth segments such as ABB’s presence in Food & Beverage. 

One of the common threads throughout the event was the Internet of Things, Services and People, which has touchpoints to all four focus areas. The ABB stand included collaborative displays from Discrete Automation and Motion, Low Voltage Products and Process Automation.

title: ABB Robotics - YuMi®, the ABB Dual Arm Robot at Automatica 2014
description:
The new era of robotic co-workers is here. YuMi® makes collaboration between humans and robots a reality.

At ABB Robotics we know that collaboration between humans and robots is going to be a large part of the future, especially when it comes to assembly of small parts and the electronics industry. At this year's Automatica we assembled more than 3,000 switches per day live at the show in a demonstration of real-life human-robot collaboration.

YuMi® is the result of years of research and development, making collaboration between humans and robots a reality. But it is also much more.

YuMi® is a vision of the future. YuMi® will change the way
we think about assembly automation. YuMi® is “you and me,”
working together to create endless possibilities.

They employ 30 ABB robots to pack the wooden panels in cartons for shipping & produce 2.6 million wardrobes per year; so they need to ensure the facility runs 24/7 with maximum uptime. title: ABB Robotics - Electric Motor Manufacturing at Baldor (ABB)
description:
For Baldor Electric Company (a member of the ABB Group), robots are part of a strategy to stay close to their customers and retain good employees by keeping manufacturing in the US and taking humans out of harm's way.

The robots are working in the Southwestern Die Casting facility—which is tasked with producing all the aluminum castings for Baldor's motors. Foundry work is dirty and dangerous, which lends itself well to robotic automation. And just down the road from Southwestern Die in Fort Smith, Baldor's newest winding facility, robots also come into play during the incredibly intricate and complex step of winding copper coils. 

"Some people might think that because ABB and Baldor are the same company now we get special treatment," says Jed Reinhard, Manager of Engineering and Maintenance for the Southwestern Die Casting facility." But the reality is that we were using ABB robots long before the acquisition and we are buying more today for the same reasons we always have: they are reliable and ABB provides great customer service—something we also value as a core mission at Baldor."

title: ABB Robotics - Handling bottled water
description:
With the help of ABB robots, bottled water provider, Oasis Water Company in Dubai UAE, has automated a once-hazardous operation and increased its production capacity so much that the firm is now the second largest 5-gallon water bottle producer in the world. 

Paul Dunlea, plant manager for the firm's Dubai facility says "Thanks to ABB robots the factory is now producing 60,000 5-gallon bottles a day. This is a huge achievement that wouldn't be possible without the robots."

With the company's previous mechanical rack loader set-up, it was able to achieve cycle times of 29-30 seconds, however thanks to ABB's robots the firm has been able to cut cycle times and increase productivity by an impressive 36%. 

In addition to helping Oasis Water gain a competitive advantage in terms of production, the ABB robots have also helped create a safer working environment for plant employees.

Read more:
http://www.abb.com/cawp/seitp202/ad3b7eb78f1241a2c125761600405715.aspx

title: Bucher Municipal - Phoenix spreading machine
description:
Bucher Phoenix ► http://winter.buchermunicipal.com/en/node/191047

High-value spreading machines for medium-size and large trucks. 
Available in container size from 4.0 m³ to 9.0 m³

Series Phoenix

The new Phoenix – a product of practical thinking, is able to optimize the development and sustainable work. Available in container sizes from 4.0 m³ to 9.0 m³, Phoenix is optimized for the carrier vehicles of all well-known truck manufacturers. A combination of modular container construction, reliable auger system, an up-to-date pre-wetted salt device, installation systems, propulsion types, sensors and controls allow an exact coordination of the winter road clearance application on roads, main roads and motorways.

The particularly high-quality finish of the Phoenix spreaders is designed for permanent use even under the severest conditions. All materials used meet the strictest quality standards. The latest robotic welding technology and advanced painting process are aimed at consistent durability and high efficiency.

The Phoenix spreaders entail a high level of reliability, a fi rst-class service and a favourable price performance ratio. The excellent equipment fi nish and the use of tried-and-tested components are a further proof of the great technical expertise built into our machines.

title: ABB Robotics - Service Support & Remote Service
description:
ABB's Remote Service concept is aimed at extending the mean time between failure of robots and robotic components. It will shorten the subsequent mean time to repair and lower the overall cost of ownership of the robot unit. 

An additional beneficiary will be the environment, with fewer service related call outs, travelling and fuel costs involved. 

With Remote Service, the robot itself automatically alerts the central database. It triggers an SMS to the on-call service engineer, who can immediately access a detailed data and error log and quickly identify the exact fault. From that moment on, ABB can support the customer remotely (in 50 percent of cases, the robot can be brought back on line without any further intervention) or through direct access to the robot's control system. If parts need to be replaced, the replacement item can either be shipped from local stores or from one of the ABB central warehouses, and a service engineer can be quickly dispatched to fit the new part.

title: Pacific Intertie – Celilo Upgrade: A lifetime with ABB
description:
From 1965, ABB has helped build this critical power transmission system, integrating renewable hydro-power from the Pacific Northwest.

During this over 50 year relationship, ABB has improved, maintained and upgraded this link a number of times, helping to ensure reliable, stable power transmission between northern Oregon and Los Angeles, California for generations.

This most recent upgrade and refurbishment replaces a number of key HVDC components and installs ABB’s world leading HVDC control and protection solution, MACH.

Archive footage courtesy of Bonneville Power Administration.

Since the acquisition of la Morella nuts in 2012, Barry Callebaut is much more than chocolate and cocoa. La Morella nuts, the artisanal nut specialist based South East of Barcelona/Spain, brought a wide array of new products into the Barry Callebaut family. Nuts in all kind of variations make a perfect addition to Barry Callebaut’s chocolate products - nut products being the second largest product segment used in the confectionery industry. Sourcing the best nuts is key to manufacture products of the highest quality and freshness. 
Watch the video impressions from a recent press trip to la Morella nuts in Reus/Spain.

title: ABB microgrid technology supports solar photovoltaic diesel power station in Australia
description:
The arid climate and blistering summers in Marble Bar helped this small mining center in remote Western Australia set a world record for heat – 160 consecutive days at 37.8°C (100°F) or above – that still stands after 90 years. The burning sun has also made Marble Bar unique in a modern sense, as the site of one of the world’s first utility-scale, high-penetration solar photovoltaic diesel power stations installed by ABB, Horizon Power and SunPower Australia. ABB’s Microgrid Plus control system and PowerStoreTM grid-stabilizing generator make Marble Bar (and its twin installation Nullagine) set new benchmarks for isolated hybrid diesel power systems with extremely high renewable energy generation and conversion (85 percent peak).
http://new.abb.com/power-generation/microgrids-solutions

title: Shore-to-ship power solution improves lives of Rotterdam residents
description: The residents of Rotterdam have long had to contend with noise and emissions from the diesel generators of ships in port. But thanks to a shore-to-ship power solution installed by ABB, the Stena line has been able to cut emissions and noise levels almost to zero, improving the lives of everyone living nearby.

title: Stay one step ahead with ABB’s GMS600 monitoring systems
description:
GMS600 is ABB’s next-generation monitoring system for generator circuit breakers (GCB) to enhance power plant reliability. Built on the already established GMS600 technology and proven Relion® Series 650, the state-of-the-art monitoring systems help optimize the use of GCBs in power plants. GMS600 offers unique features such as remaining time before service is needed, SF6 gas monitoring and trending (GMS600-G) and temperature monitoring of primary conductors (GMS600-GT).

More information: http://new.abb.com/high-voltage/gcb/accessories/monitoring-system-gms600

title: ABB enables Europe’s longest HVDC link: NordLink
description: Stretching over 623 kilometers, the NordLink HVDC system between Norway and Germany is Europe’s longest HVDC interconnection. The link permits Germany to integrate more volatile renewable energy into the grid and to use Norway’s hydropower as backup when needed. This will help meet European Union targets for CO2 reduction. The transfer capacity is 1,400 MW at ±525 kV.

title: ABB's engineering, sourcing and manufacturing operation in San Luis Potosí, Mexico
description: Opened in 2009, the ABB location in San Luis Potosi integrates engineering, sourcing and manufacturing for all of ABB's divisions. The campus-like facility was designed to encourage the sharing of ideas, technologies, resources and best practices between the divisions. Currently, the facility manufactures and assembles high-, medium- and low-voltage products; robotics, and control systems and automation products. The facility also offers standard and specialized engineering services, and is home to a customer service center supporting utility, industrial and commercial customers designed to increase the effectiveness and efficiency of ABB automation equipment.

title: The most efficient power plant in the world runs with ABB Symphony Plus
description:
Heat and light are provided to 20,000 Danish households by the Avedøre combined heat and power plant, the most efficient power plant in the world. The site, located just outside Copenhagen, produces heat and electricity from coal, petroleum, biomass and by-products of waste. The plant runs efficiently and produces a reliable power supply thanks to ABB Symphony Plus distributed control system and its simple, self-explanatory HMI S+ Operations.

The Avedøre site is part of Denmark’s strategy to achieve 100 percent renewable energy generation by 2050. The plant is expected to be almost fully converted to green energy over the coming years.

http://new.abb.com/power-generation/symphony-plus

title: ABB Welding Diodes – as you have never seen them before
description:
Almost every second car driving in Europe is welded using ABB welding diodes. ABB welding diodes are presspack devices specially designed for medium and high-frequency welding equipment and optimized for high-current rectifiers. ABB’s high-frequency welding diodes operate at up to 10 kilohertz, allowing for smaller and lighter welding transformers and thus enabling robots to move easier and faster.

A product family within ABB’s Semiconductors range used in welding rectifiers mainly for sheet welding in the automotive industry.
http://new.abb.com/semiconductors/Diodes/welding-diodes

title: TEMS Pocket LTE MIMO cell TX antenna balancing
description: Can you be sure your MIMO antennas are working correctly? A new LTE data view “eNB TX Antenna Difference” in TEMS Pocket indicates the difference in transmit power between the TX antennas of an eNodeB. These measurements can be used to determine in real time if a newly deployed site has a problem with one of the TX antennas. Check out this video, and read more about TEMS Pocket and our other TEMS products at our website: www.ascom.com/networktesting

title: Innovative hybrid grid energy storage and diesel generation system for AusNet Services
description:
In Victoria, Australia, a hybrid grid energy storage and diesel generation system (GESS) is being provided by ABB and Samsung to strengthen the state’s distribution network. The system is made up of ABB’s battery energy storage (PowerStoreTM) and Samsung’s diesel generator. Designed for independent isolated networks, often referred to as microgrids, the PowerStore grid-stabilizing generator has proved itself ideal for providing additional power capacity during high-demand periods, also in a traditional distribution network. This means the diesel generator only needs to be activated on limited occasions, allowing environmental impact and fuel costs to be reduced.

title: ABB microgrid controls help optimize renewable integration
description:
ABB's modular and scalable control solution enables the integration of renewables into microgrids serving remote locations like islands. This helps maximize the contribution of clean energy sources and reduces dependence on fossil fuels while maintaining grid stability. Dynamic control is an important function given the intermittent nature of renewables like wind which can cause frequency and voltage fluctuations that can destabilize the microgrid and even result in power outages. Another example of how ABB enables power and productivity for a better world.

description:
It is based on ABB's well-proven GIS technology and can be produced at the same speed as a conventional GIS but has a considerably shorter installation time which is less than 50 percent of typical GIS installations.

The integrated GIS is the perfect solution for customers in need of a substation that can be quickly energized for grid expansions, for backup or emergency power needs and for short installation time requirements. It is also ideal for applications in the oil, gas, and mining industries.

title: The hidden systems that keep Dubai moving
description: Dubai's driverless metro network moves over 140,000 people per day in a city where summer day-time temperatures regularly exceed 40 degrees Celsius. Reliability of power supply is essential. ABB's Substation Control and Monitoring Systems (SCMS) and low-voltage products are designed to increase energy efficiency and grid reliability for the entire electrical network. The substations also feature fault recorders and tariff metering systems to carefully monitor performance and ensure smooth operation.

title: Sunliquid® Wins IKU German Innovation Prize for Climate & Environment
description:
Clariant's sunliquid® technology was awarded the 2015 German Innovation Prize for Climate and Environment (Der Deutsche Innovationspreis für Klima und Umwelt or IKU).

The biotechnology process enables production of ethanol from renewable lignocellulosic resources, such as agricultural residues. These resources don't compete with food and feed crops because they are created in sufficient quantities worldwide as a by-product of current agricultural practices, as in the case of straw from cereal production.

In short, sunliquid® allows for the production of bioethanol in a sustainable and economical way -- a way to produce biofuel without compromising our food resources in a world with ever-growing energy demands. 

Learn more about the Innovation prize: http://www.iku-innovationspreis.de/iku-innopreis/index.php

Learn more about Sunliquid here: Clariant.com/sunliquid

A special thanks to IKU for producing this video.

title: ABB's offering for Klabin, Brazil's largest paper manufacturer
description: Klabin SA, one of Brazil's largest paper manufacturers and exporters, uses ABB's electrical and automation systems to improve energy efficiency and industrial productivity in a new paperboard line, which has boosted output of its liquid packaging board product by 50 percent. ABB is supplying new automation systems based on its System 800xA platform and upgrades to existing systems for a uniform operating environment. ABB will also supply electrical systems including a 69 kV substation, transformers, refiner motors and protection equipment, process electrification, and paper machinery drives. ABB monitoring and quality systems ensure top performance of paper and pulping machinery.

description: The Flooring Systems division offers a broad and attractive range of environmentally friendly linoleum, high-quality vinyl floors, entrance flooring systems for cleaning and drying shoes, carpet tiles, needle felt, and Flotex, the washable textile flooring. Thanks to their excellent technical properties and attractive design, these flooring solutions are invariably the first choice for public buildings, department stores, hospitals and healthcare facilities, schools, libraries, commercial offices, leisure centers, hotels, restaurants, and cafeterias and are also used in the residential market. With a market share of over 65 percent, Forbo is the world market leader in linoleum. Under the brand name Eurocol, Flooring Systems also provides ready-made adhesives for flooring installations and ceramic tiles, as well as leveling compounds for the construction industry.

title: ABB Smart Grid Laboratory in Dalmine, Italy
description:
Inaugurated just over a year ago, the specially designed ABB Smart Grid Laboratory in Dalmine, Italy uses real components to simulate the operation of electricity distribution networks, making it an invaluable aid in smart grid development. It enables the behavioral simulation of the protection and automation systems to detect and select only the grid section affected by a malfunction. It helps verify that the healthy portion of the grid is properly reconfigured within a few milliseconds using the selective logic programmed in the protection relays, thus enhancing reliability and efficiency.

More information: http://www.abb.com/cawp/seitp202/7bcaf145fe052ca0c1257e49003437af.aspx

title: CALIDA The Beauty of a Moment - Herbst/Winter Film 2013
description:
CALIDA - www.calida.com
Das Leben besteht aus unzähligen kleinen Momenten & Anekdoten, die unser Leben prägen und liebenswert machen. Das kann ein gemütlicher Plausch mit der besten Freundin sein, "Nickerchen" auf dem Sofa oder ein Solo auf der Luftgitarre, wenn keiner guckt. Halt das, wonach einem gerade ist, damit man sich wohlfühlt. Oder anders ausgedrückt: Leben, ganz im Sinne unseres Claims. "Naturally me. Ich bin wie ich bin. Und wie."
Mehr über die neue Kollektion auf www.calida.com

title: CALIDA Spring/Summer 2014
description:
www.calida.com
Unser Frühling/Sommer 2014 Film steht ganz im Zeichen neu erwachender Lebensfreude: sich kleine Fluchten aus dem Alltagstrott zu gönnen, seine Liebsten zu schnappen und kleine Auszeiten zu zelebrieren. Das Leben in allen Facetten spüren und vor allem auf ganz unterschiedliche Weise geniessen. "Naturally me. Ich bin wie ich bin. Und wie."
...
Our spring/summer 14 movie is all about a newly awakened joy for life: indulging in short escapes from daily routines, or grabbing loved ones to celebrate a quick time-out. In short, living every aspect of life to the fullest and enjoying it in as many ways as possible. 
"Naturally me. I am how I am. Totally."

title: People - change is inevitable
description:
Traditionally well-being and societal progress has been linked to the economic system of a given country, yet happiness is unique to the individual and isn't necessarily determined by income. 
________________________________________
By 2020 Google estimates that there will be five billion of us accessing the internet on over 50 billion devices. The ease in which information circulates globally means that the same knowledge is available providing us all equal opportunity.
Innovation and the will to change are important prerequisites for a nation that wants to grow and prosper. Identifying and understanding the importance of future popular trends can give decisive insights into the direction in which technology is developing, as well as the general shift in the paradigm of society.

Julius Baer Next Generation -- People identifies the positive trends associated with people and their way of life, bringing you creative and attractive investment solutions for the future.

http://www.juliusbaer.com/people

title: Teaser: Skin Odyssey - Givaudan Active Beauty 360 Virtual Reality Experience

This virtual reality experience will transport you on an amazing journey into the beauty of the skin.  During this incredible journey, you will be temporarily reduced to a microscopic scale to take place onboard a Spherulites™ capsule, a technological vector which will drive you into the layers of the skin, down to the extra-cellular matrix.

The Skin Odyssey application can be downloaded for free to your mobile device (iOS and Android versions available) through this link: https://www.givaudan.com/specials/active-beauty-skin-odyssey

title: Julius Baer Next Generation iApp
description:
Julius Baer Next Generation aims to prepare you for the opportunities and challenges that lie ahead of us all. Learn more about the trends shaping our future and find out how you can adapt and embrace change as it happens.
________________________________________

The Julius Baer Next Generation App comprises an interactive virtual magazine, three innovative research studies and a recommendation booklet (the latter downloadable in Switzerland only).

The Virtual Magazine
Explore how the future can make you happy by losing yourself in this interactive virtual magazine. Click on its various features: think, listen and play, to discover some interesting and thought-provoking ideas presented in a very unique way.

The Research Studies
The only constant in life is change. The world advances and personal circumstances invariably evolve over time: technology replaces jobs, equality increases and we grow older. In the three studies: Happiness, Growing up and Flat world, realise how happiness might be sustained over a lifetime.

The Recommendation Booklet (for Switzerland only)
Well-being relies on many factors from time spent with loved ones to good health and financial security. While money itself might not make us happy, the wealthier we are the better positioned we are to deploy our wealth to improve well-being. Julius Baer supports you and your family by offering diverse solutions to optimise quality of life.

http://www.juliusbaer.com/app

Die globale Reichweite der Organisation ermöglicht die Nähe zu dynamischen Märkten und macht Forbo zur ersten Wahl als lokalen Partner für Kunden mit ähnlichem globalem Anspruch. Die Qualität, die Langlebigkeit und die Leistungsfähigkeit unserer Produkte und Systeme entsprechen der Qualität und Dauerhaftigkeit der Beziehungen zu unseren Geschäftspartnern.

title: Forbo Corporate Group Movie (2014)
description:
Forbo is a leading producer of floor coverings, building and construction adhesives, as well as power transmission and conveyor belt solutions. The company employs more than 5,100 people and has an international network of 24 production and distribution companies, 6 fabrication centers and 42 sales organizations in a total of 36 countries worldwide. Forbo is headquartered in Baar in the canton of Zug, Switzerland, and quoted on the SIX Swiss Stock Exchange.

Forbo is a global player, and supplies with its two divisions a wide range of industries. The Group's global reach means that it is close to dynamic markets and makes it the first choice as a local partner for customers that have similar global requirements. The quality, longevity, and performance of our products and systems reflect the quality and stability of our relations with our business partners.

title: Forbo Movment Systems (2014 - German)
description: Der Geschäftsbereich Movement Systems nimmt weltweit eine führende Stellung ein als Anbieter von erstklassigen Antriebsriemen, hochwertigen Transport- und Prozessbändern, Kunststoffmodulbändern sowie Zahn- und Flachriemen aus synthetischen Materialien. Bekannt sind diese Produkte unter dem Markennamen Siegling. Sie werden für unterschiedlichste Anwendungen in der Industrie und in Handels- und Dienstleistungsbetrieben eingesetzt, zum Beispiel als Förder- und Prozessbänder in der Lebensmittelindustrie, als Laufbänder in Fitnessstudios oder als Flachriemen in Briefverteilanlagen.

title: Forbo Flooring Systems auf einen Blick (2014)
description:
BeschreibungDer Geschäftsbereich Flooring Systems bietet ein breites und attraktives Produktangebot von umweltfreundlichem Linoleum, hochwertigen Vinylbodenbelägen, Sauberlaufsystemen, Teppichfliesen, Nadelvliesbelägen sowie Flotex, dem waschbaren Textilboden, an. Dank ihrer ausgezeichneten Gebrauchseigenschaften und ihres attraktiven Designs sind diese Bodenbeläge die erste Wahl für öffentliche Gebäude, Warenhäuser, Krankenhäuser und Einrichtungen im Gesundheitsbereich, für Schulen, Bibliotheken, Geschäftsräume, Freizeitzentren, Hotels, Restaurants und Cafeterias sowie für Anwendungen im Wohnbereich. Mit einem Marktanteil von über 65% ist Forbo weltweiter Marktführer bei Linoleum.

Flooring Systems bietet unter dem Markennamen Eurocol auch Fertigklebstoffe zum Verlegen von Bodenbelägen und Keramikfliesen sowie Nivelier- und Ausgleichsmassen für die Bauindustrie an.

title: Forbo Movement Systems (2014)
description: BeschreibungMovement Systems is a global industry leader providing top-quality power transmission belts, sophisticated conveyor and processing belts, as well as plastic modular, timing, and flat belts made of synthetic materials. These products are known under the brand name Siegling. They are used in a wider range of applications in the industry, trade, and the service sector, for example as conveyor and processing belts in the food industry, as treadmill belts in fitness studios, and flat belts in mail distribution centers.

title: Active arcing fault protection system for dry-type transformers
description: An arcing fault can have fatal consequences for the operator and strongly damage the system and even the building. To effectively prevent from such consequences, ABB recommends the use of an active arcing fault protection system which has already proven to be successful in switchgears. Reliable protection of people and property with the Ultra-Fast Earthing Switch (UFESTM) is now also available for dry-type transformers.

description: Royal Caribbean's Oasis of the Seas is the largest cruise ship ever built. It is equipped with an ABB power and propulsion system, setting a new standard for fuel efficiency, ship maneuverability and passenger comfort.

title: Forbo Flooring Systems (2014)
description: The Flooring Systems division offers a broad and attractive range of environmentally friendly linoleum, high-quality vinyl floors, entrance flooring systems for cleaning and drying shoes, carpet tiles, needle felt, and Flotex, the washable textile flooring. Thanks to their excellent technical properties and attractive design, these flooring solutions are invariably the first choice for public buildings, department stores, hospitals and healthcare facilities, schools, libraries, commercial offices, leisure centers, hotels, restaurants, and cafeterias and are also used in the residential market. With a market share of over 65 percent, Forbo is the world market leader in linoleum. Under the brand name Eurocol, Flooring Systems also provides ready-made adhesives for flooring installations and ceramic tiles, as well as leveling compounds for the construction industry.

title: Making cables for the world´s power systems
description: ABB is one of the world's leading suppliers of high-voltage cable systems and installation services, providing unrivaled expertise in a wide range of segments including city center infeeds with undergrounding cables, oil and gas platform power links, interconnections and the integration of renewables, such as Offshore Wind. Quality, reliability and long trouble-free operating life are the hallmarks of ABB High Voltage Cables. They are the result of extensive and ongoing product development and manufacture at an ABB factory of world-class standard. Drawing on 140 years of cable expertise, ABB has a unique track record in the development of new AC and DC cable technologies and solutions for submarine and underground cable applications. For more information, please visit: http://www.abb.com/cables

title: Urinal with self sustaining power supply - Functionality
description:
Every flush generates electricity. In the video, you can see how a small generator dispenses with the need for a mains connection and battery operation on Geberit urinal ceramic appliances with an integrated urinal flush control. For operation, this means less maintenance work as there is no battery change, independence from the power supply system and easier installation. For the environment, this means less battery waste and more sustainability. Good, eh?

Jede Spülung erzeugt Strom. Im Film seht Ihr, wie ein kleiner Generator Netzanschluss und Batteriebetrieb bei den Geberit Urinalkeramiken mit integrierter Steuerung überflüssig macht. Für den Betrieb heisst das: Weniger Wartungsaufwand, da Batteriewechsel entfallen, Stromnetzunabhängigkeit und einfachere Montage. Für die Umwelt bedeutet das: Weniger Batterieabfall und mehr Nachhaltigkeit. Gut, oder?

ID 2109735

If you need technical support, please contact the local technical support team. 

Please note: Videos do not replace installation manuals. For installation purposes, please use the respective Geberit installation manuals.

For more information about Geberit visit http://www.geberit.com

Der Geschäftsbereich Flooring Systems bietet ein breites und attraktives Produktangebot von umweltfreundlichem Linoleum, hochwertigen Vinylbodenbelägen, Sauberlaufsystemen, Teppichfliesen, Nadelvliesbelägen sowie Flotex, dem waschbaren Textilboden, an. Dank ihrer ausgezeichneten Gebrauchseigenschaften und ihres attraktiven Designs sind diese Bodenbeläge die erste Wahl für öffentliche Gebäude, Warenhäuser, Krankenhäuser und Einrichtungen im Gesundheitsbereich, für Schulen, Bibliotheken, Geschäftsräume, Freizeitzentren, Hotels, Restaurants und Cafeterias sowie für Anwendungen im Wohnbereich. Mit einem Marktanteil von über 65% ist Forbo weltweiter Marktführer bei Linoleum.

Flooring Systems bietet unter dem Markennamen Eurocol auch Fertigklebstoffe zum Verlegen von Bodenbelägen und Keramikfliesen sowie Nivelier- und Ausgleichsmassen für die Bauindustrie an.

title: Forbo Movement Systems (2013)
description: Movement Systems is a global industry leader providing top-quality power transmission belts, sophisticated conveyor and processing belts, as well as plastic modular, timing, and flat belts made of synthetic materials. These products are known under the brand name Siegling. They are used in a wider range of applications in the industry, trade, and the service sector, for example as conveyor and processing belts in the food industry, as treadmill belts in fitness studios, and flat belts in mail distribution centers.

title: Tailored service solutions for every transformer - ABB Engineering solutions
description:
Ageing assets, rising energy demand, and the need to deliver without unplanned outages are some of the challenges utilities and industries are facing today around the world. This video presents different ABB service offerings to assess the operational condition of transformers, provide recommendations to mitigate risks and enhance the performance of aged equipment to meet possible new requirements. For more information, please visit:
http://www.abb.com/transformers

title: Fly over the fields of lavender - Givaudan 360° video
description:
Fly over the fields of lavender with us in our first ever 360° video! Stunning new technology allows you to travel to the southern region of France to explore the artistry that goes into distilling our precious lavender essence. Givaudan explores a new, modern approach to share our ethical sourcing programme by taking you behind the scenes to discover the world of lavender from another perspective. 

Learn more on www.givaudan.com

Viewing options to get the most out of your 360° experience:

-  On PC/Mac: Press play. Use your mouse to click and drag or click the arrows in the top left corner.
 
-  iPhone: View the video from the YouTube app and tilt your phone to enjoy the full 360° effects around you.
 
-  Android only: View the video from the YouTube app. If you have a Google Cardboard, click the Cardboard logo and watch the video in stereoscopy.

title: Forbo Flooring Systems (2013)
description: The Flooring Systems division offers a broad and attractive range of environmentally friendly linoleum, high-quality vinyl floors, entrance flooring systems for cleaning and drying shoes, carpet tiles, needle felt, and Flotex, the washable textile flooring. Thanks to their excellent technical properties and attractive design, these flooring solutions are invariably the first choice for public buildings, department stores, hospitals and healthcare facilities, schools, libraries, commercial offices, leisure centers, hotels, restaurants, and cafeterias and are also used in the residential market. With a market share of over 65 percent, Forbo is the world market leader in linoleum. Under the brand name Eurocol, Flooring Systems also provides ready-made adhesives for flooring installations and ceramic tiles, as well as leveling compounds for the construction industry.

title: Plantasens Olive Active HP – Slow down the clock on skin aging
description:
Skin’s natural aging process cannot be stopped –
but Plantasens Olive Active HP can help slow it down significantly

Over time the layers of our skin are affected by oxygen and UV rays. The epidermis becomes thinner and rough, the dermis loses its structure and resilience.
Plantasens® Olive Active HP – a powerful anti-aging active ingredient derived from olive – acts as a protective shield.

For more information visit:
http://www.clariant.com/en/Innovation/Innovation-Spotlight-Videos/Plantasens-Olive-Active-HP

title: Inside Feintool – Diverse Training Opportunities
description:
Feintool is investing in its own young talent: training in a total of ten occupations in the company, which specializes in fineblanking and forming, is available worldwide. Each year, around 80 young people are trained up to become proven specialists in their field. Training takes place at the company's sites in Switzerland, Germany and the USA. 

One such trainee is toolmaker trainee Thanujan in Lyss, Switzerland. With Feintool's technical training he found exactly what he was looking for. Whether focusing on tasks for his own customer project in tool maintenance or working in a team with other trainees in the workshop, the variety throughout his training at Feintool motivates him just as much as his hobby, Thai boxing.

Find out more about exciting vocational training opportunities at Feintool: http://www.feintool.com/en/careers/vocational-training.html 

The Feintool world: http://www.feintool.com/en/company.html

description:

title: Geberit Urinalsystem - Commercial (eng)
description:
The new Geberit urinal system offers a suitable solution for virtually any construction task. The comprehensive product range means that the planning, installation and maintenance of urinal systems are all greatly simplified. The Geberit urinal system offers the opportunity of implementing an economical water flush to an individually programmable hybrid mode or operation entirely without water. Central elements are the two rimless Geberit urinal ceramics, both of which were developed by Geberit. They have an inner geometry that perfectly matches the spray head – which has also been newly developed – and ensures an optimal flushing out of the urinal, even at the smallest flush volume of 0.5 liters. An essential advantage is the fact that the ceramics do not need to be removed for maintenance because it’s easy to access all components.

title: Precision high speed picking, packing and palletizing solution from ABB
description:
With the following, picking, pakcing and palletizing application, ABB is demonstrating a factory automation solution, integrated industrial productivity and energy efficiency, for the solution we are using a complete portfolio of discrete automation and motion components from ABB. These components include robots, drives, AC500 PLCs, PROFINET I/O, Motors, Jokab Safety systems and low voltage equipment's.

These components can be combined to satisfy the requirements of many varied and complex automation applications. One such application is the pick, pack and palletizing operation in which products of varying size and shape are presented for packaging at random intervals and in random orientations. Human operators are generally fast as well as versatile when it comes to picking and placing items such as biscuits, chocolates, electronic components and pharmaceuticals. It is precisely because of this dexterity, combined with the need for high line productivity (average throughput rates of several hundred packs per minute are quite typical) that such huge demands are made on the agility and capability of automation equipment for this kind of work.

A typical high-speed picking application involves locating, inspecting, picking and placing products in boxes or into an in-feed of another machine. To optimize system throughput, high speed picking generally occurs "on the fly," allowing a robot to seamlessly move from one pick sequence to another without wasting precious time. While advances in robotic technology have offered the flexibility needed to cope with these variations, true success lies in the selection and interaction of all system components. The following paragraphs outline a recommended ABB solution that can effectively perform this task.

title: Geberit Urinalsystem - Commercial
description:
Das neue Geberit Urinalsystem bietet für nahezu jedes Anwendungsszenario die passende Lösung. Das umfassende Sortiment vereinfacht Planung, Installation und Unterhalt von Urinalanlagen. Zur Auswahl stehen eine sparsame Wasserspülung, eine individuelle Programmierung mit Intervallspülung oder der Betrieb der Urinalanlagen ganz ohne Wasser. Zentrale Elemente sind die spülrandlosen Geberit Urinalkeramiken. Deren Innengeometrie ist exakt auf den neu entwickelten Sprühkopf abgestimmt und gewährleistet selbst bei kleinsten Spülmengen von 0,5 Litern eine optimale Ausspülung. Ein entscheidender Vorteil ist, dass die Urinalkeramiken für Wartungsarbeiten nicht entfernt werden müssen, da alle Komponenten leicht zugänglich sind.

title: Tubairless® Airless system for creams and semi solids
description:
Tubairless® protects sensitive pharmaceutical cream formulas during utilization, maximizing stability and shelf life.

Tubairless is an airless system specifically designed for cream applications that combines the advantages of a flexible tube with the performance of airless packaging.

Because of its unique technology that integrates a soft plastic pouch sealed within a tube, Tubairless dispenses cream formulations while ensuring no air-intake or product flow back, protecting its contents from moisture gain or loss as well as certain types of contamination during product use.
® Trademark registered in many countries and owned by Pumpart

For more information visit: http://www.clariant.com/tubairless
