title: Interroll Distribution Center FritoLay
description:
Interroll flow storage systems are space saving and compact - there are no intermediate aisles, but only one input and one output aisle. All products are available by the shortest route in one aisle.
It is the fastest and most cost-effective distribution system for assorted products.
Interroll flow storage provides simple, structured stock control with unlimited flexibility and tailor made design and layout.
A conventional system needs double as much space and more aisles, meaning 7 to 8 times longer travel routes for the operators.
Consequently, more forklift truck drivers are needed and retrieval and dispatch takes much more time.

title: Nestlé develops sustainable water use for Vietnam coffee production
description:
Vietnam is the world's leading coffee producer and the largest global supplier of Robusta coffee for Nestlé. Nestlé Vietnam's Farmer Connect Programme currently supports over 12,000 coffee farmers, providing them technical assistance and training increasing productivity.

Groundwater, the main source of coffee irrigation during the dry months in Vietnam, is declining rapidly due to overuse, and could potentially affect coffee productivity in the long term. In order to develop sustainable water use practices, Nestlé partnered with the Swiss Agency for Development and Cooperation (SDC), the Western Highlands Agriculture and Forestry Science Institute (WASI), International Water Management Institute (IWMI) and Embden, Drishaus and Epping Consulting (EDE) to conduct a study on the irrigation practices in coffee farming in the Dak Lak Province.

title: Disease modeling to inform clinical trial simulation
description:

title: New media: drivers of democratization and development?
description:
Held on December 2, 2011 in Basel, the 16th annual symposium of the Novartis Foundation for Sustainable Development (NFSD) discussed the potential of Information and Communication Technologies (ICT) for democratization and development. Speakers looked at the role of Facebook and other social media platforms in the Arab revolutions and analyzed the impact of mobile phone penetration into the African continent. Moreover, experts looked at the potential of technologies to help improve access to quality healthcare services in developing countries and ultimately contribute to the Millennium Development Goals. 

Learn more at: www.novartisfoundation.org/symposium

© 2011 Novartis AG

title: Roger Federer and his new friend.
Access to medical innovation in cancer care
description:
Despite major breakthroughs in cancer care throughout the last 50 years, achieving universal access to medical innovation and quality healthcare is a global challenge. As a leader in oncology, with a strong heritage and depth of knowledge in the area, Roche understands its responsibility as a key partner both globally and locally. Roche aims to ensure that every patient who needs its medicines has access to them and is committed to work with partners to find new approaches to ensuring future patient access to quality cancer care globally.
Peter Braun, Global Head of Public Affairs at Roche, gives his perspective on this topic in this video.

Check out our ASCO website where we are reflecting on our own heritage at this year's 50th ASCO annual meeting: www.roche.com/ASCO

More videos from Roche on http://www.youtube.com/user/roche

title: Roche in Spinal Muscular Atrophy
description:
Spinal Muscular Atrophy (SMA) is the leading genetic cause of death in infants and toddlers and is associated with a high rate of childhood mortality in its most severe forms. In a collaboration between Roche Pharma Research and Early Development (pRED), PTC Pharmaceuticals and the SMA Foundation, orally available molecules were discovered that may specifically correct a certain genetic defect in this devastating disorder. In mouse models of SMA, the compounds allow the animals to survive, grow and develop a normal neuromuscular system. We are working together to make a difference for SMA patients and their families.
For more information: www.roche.com 

More Videos from Roche on http://www.youtube.com/user/roche

title: Drawn to Science: Clinical trials
description:
Clinical trials are essential to providing the scientific data to determine whether new drugs, diagnostics or procedures are both safe and effective when used to diagnose and treat people. 

Carefully conducted clinical trials are performed in human volunteers to provide answers to important questions. Every new treatment is usually tested in three phases of clinical trials before regulatory agencies consider it safe and effective. Depending on product type and development stage, investigators initially enroll volunteers and/or patients into small pilot studies and subsequently conduct progressively larger scale comparative studies. As positive safety and efficacy data are gathered, the number of patients typically increases. Clinical trials can vary in size, and can involve a single research entity in one country or multiple entities in multiple countries. 

For more information about clinical trials at Roche visit http://www.roche.com/research_and_development/who_we_are_how_we_work/clinical_trials.htm

title: Multiple Sclerosis: Understanding the roles of B cells
description:
Multiple Sclerosis (MS) affects 2.3 million people world-wide and is a leading cause of neurological disability. Recent clinical evidence suggests that B cells contribute to both the inflammatory and neurodegenerative aspects of the disease. This film by Roche illustrates how B cells are believed to play a role in the origination as well as the development of MS.

title: Oncology - Decision making in an increasingly complex treatment environment
description:
The last 50 years over which the "American Society of Clinical Oncology" (ASCO) has existed has seen huge advances in our understanding of cancer, and therefore also in how we can treat the disease. While this is obviously overwhelmingly positive for patients and physicians, this has also meant that treatment decisions are getting ever-more complex.
Niko André, Head of Global Medical Affairs Oncology at Roche, gives his perspective on how decision-making by physicians has become complicated these days, and how this can be addressed.

Check out our conference website where we are reflecting on our own heritage at this year's 50th ASCO annual meeting: www.roche.com/ASCO

More videos from Roche on http://www.youtube.com/user/roche

Flexible building use is another important feature: Office spaces are modular in design, for example, and can be adapted to meet the required mix of single offices, open plan offices, and meeting rooms.

Moreover, Building 1 will be a most energy-efficient building, i.e. heated with waste heat and cooled with groundwater, while setting new energy efficiency standards with its innovative façade and LED lighting.

title: Roche Diagnostics: Der Standort Rotkreuz
description:
Die Roche Diagnostics International AG im zugerischen Rotkreuz ist einer der weltweit führenden Anbieter von diagnostischen Systemlösungen für Kliniken, Labors und Arztpraxen. Die Roche Diagnostics International AG wurde 1969 gegründet und hat heute über 2'000 Mitarbeitende aus rund 50 Nationen. Am Standort sind alle zentralen Funktionen des Diagnostics Geschäfts vereint. Mit den beiden Bereichen Global Platforms & Support und Global Instruments Operations ist die Roche Diagnostics International ein Kompetenzzentrum für modernes Gerätedesign und Download eines der weltweit führenden Entwicklungs-, Produktions- und Servicezentren für Instrumenten- und Softwaresysteme, die in der In-Vitro-Diagnostik und Download in der bioanalytischen Forschung eingesetzt werden. Mehr Informationen über den Standort Rotkreuz: http://www.roche.ch/standorte/rotkreuz.htm

title: Understanding more about why quality of life matters
description:
The film provides explanations on what quality of life really means, how a physician can make a difference to a patient's quality of life and the challenges associated with measuring the impact of treatments on quality of life. Learn about specific examples of what quality of life means for patients with breast cancer and why measuring quality of life is difficult in oncology and for clinical trials. 

A physician perspective on quality of life, with opinion and comment from Dr Arthur Katz (Medical Oncology Service Coordinator), Sírio Libanês (Hospital Oncology Center, São Paulo, Brazil), Dr Eva Ciruelos (Breast Cancer Unit Coordinator, Medical Oncology Service, Hospital 12 de Octubre, Madrid), and Professor Lesley Fallowfield, (Director, Professor of Psycho-Oncology, Sussex Health Outcomes Research & Education in Cancer, University of Sussex, UK).

title: Oerlikon Systems: Hexagon Movie
Tamika, Gebärmutterhalskrebs-Überlebende
description:
Tamika hatte Gebärmutterhalskrebs und gilt seit zwölf Jahren als geheilt. Seit sie ihre Behandlung im Jahr 2001 beendete, engagiert sich Tamika in einer Patientenorganisation für Gebärmutterhalskrebs. Sie ist ausserdem Mitglied einer Gruppe von Überlebenden von Gebärmutterhalskrebs.
"Gebärmutterhalskrebs ist die einzige Krebserkrankung, der man vorbeugen kann. Denn es gibt nicht nur Mittel zur Prävention, sondern auch zum Nachweis. Frauen müssen sich selbst proaktiv für ihre Gesundheit einsetzen. Sie müssen über die vorhandenen Mittel und Wege informiert werden. Nur so können wir den Gebärmutterhalskrebs besiegen."

Warum wir jeden Tag zur Arbeit kommen: http://www.roche.com/de/about_roche/our_purpose.htm

Mehr Videos von Roche: http://www.youtube.com/user/roche

title: Vivian, Brustkrebs-Überlebende
description:
Vivian ist Mutter von drei Kindern und hatte metastasierenden Brustkrebs, der erfolgreich behandelt wurde. "Mein erster Gedanke nach der Diagnose metastasierender Brustkrebs war 'Ich werde sterben'. Ich war erst 29 Jahre alt - zu jung für eine solche Krankheit. Eine solche Diagnose verändert dein Leben, in dessen Hektik du dir manchmal nicht mal die Zeit für eine Tasse Kaffee gönnst. Du denkst: 'Was habe ich aus meinem Leben gemacht? Und wie wird es weiter gehen?' Dann beginnst du, jeden Augenblick deines Lebens zu geniessen. Es ist eine Tatsache, dass meine medizinische Behandlung mich am Leben erhält. Ich geniesse jeden Augenblick meines Lebens."

Warum wir jeden Tag zur Arbeit kommen: http://www.roche.com/de/about_roche/our_purpose.htm

Mehr Videos von Roche: http://www.youtube.com/user/roche

title: Panalpina’s end-to-end e-commerce solution
description: Logistics is changing -- rather than simply transporting goods from A to B, there is a growing trend for partners to take more ownership and responsibility for their customer's supply chains. As Value-Added Services (VAS), Panalpina now offers a full end-to-end e-commerce solution to its customers. That combined with the company’s global footprint is a unique offering, enabling customers to sell online and expand to new markets fast and easy – as it has never been possible before. Panalpina’s e-commerce suite includes the provision of webshops, order management system, logistics fulfillment services with last mile deliveries and all the additional components that one needs for successful online trading.

title: Sophia Chao, Regional Senior Project Manager, Roche Molecular Diagnostics
description:
Sophia Chao ist hauptsächlich für die Entwicklung wichtiger regionaler Projekte und für die lokale Umsetzung wegweisender geschäftlicher Programme für den Bereich Molekulardiagnostik verantwortlich. "Ich schätze das Gebiet der Diagnostik, weil es sowohl den ersten Schritt als auch den zentralen Aspekt der Patientenbetreuung darstellt. Ohne Diagnostik würden Kliniker und Patienten im Dunkeln tappen, was das Kranksein unerträglich machen könnte. Ein besseres Wissen über Krankheiten ist nicht nur für Ärzte wichtig, sondern es gibt auch Patienten mehr Rückhalt. Ich habe selber HIV-infizierte Patienten gesehen, die ihre Messwerte für die Viruslast interpretieren konnten und dadurch Vertrauen in die Wirksamkeit ihrer Therpie erhielten."

Warum wir jeden Tag zur Arbeit kommen: http://www.roche.com/de/about_roche/our_purpose.htm

Mehr Videos von Roche: http://www.youtube.com/user/roche

title: Episodio 7: Login su Il mio conto
description:
http://www.sunrise.ch/il-mio-conto 
Sunrise funziona così: semplice, comprensibile, esauriente. Ed è così che deve essere il servizio per i nostri clienti Sunrise. "Funziona così" ci dà proprio questo: con i nostri video "Funziona così" Alex & Lara vi mostrano e vi presentano nei dettagli le installazioni, le spiegazioni dei prodotti, la risoluzione dei guasti e molto altro ancora, in modo chiaro e comprensibile. In Il mio conto avete quindi la possibilità di visualizzare le vostre spese correnti e le vostre fatture, di configurare un controllo dei costi e di aggiungere o disattivare le opzioni al vostro abbonamento; fate tutto in piena autonomia.

title: Episode 7: Login Mon compte
description:

Sunrise, comment ça marche: c'est simple, compréhensible et détaillé. Il doit en être de même pour notre service destiné à nos clients Sunrise. C'est exactement ce que fait «comment ça marche». Grâce à nos vidéos «Comment ça marche», Alex et Lara vous montrent et vous apprennent comment effectuer une installation, résoudre une panne, vous présentent des produits, etc., étape par étape et de manière claire. Vous pouvez également regarder la vidéo sur un smartphone si vous n'avez pas encore d'accès à Internet.
Dans cet épisode, nous vous présentons la procédure de connexion au portail Mon compte de Sunrise. Alex vous montre étape par étape comment vous connecter à Mon compte. Dans Mon compte, vous pouvez alors consulter vous-même vos coûts actuels, vos factures, configurer le contrôle des coûts, mais aussi activer des options pour votre abonnement ou les désactiver.

title: Roche Diagnostics: the Rotkreuz site
description:
Roche Diagnostics International Ltd, located in Rotkreuz in the canton of Zug, is one of the world's leading providers of diagnostic system solutions for clinics, laboratories and doctors' offices. The company was founded in 1969 and today employs over 2,000 people from around 50 countries. With the two functions of Global Platforms & Support and Global Instruments Operations, it is a center of expertise in modern instrument design and is one of the world's foremost locations for the development, production and servicing of instrument and software systems that are used in in vitro diagnostics and bioanalytical research. title: Rob Waller, Simplification Centre, on the impact of simplified contracts
description:
The Director of the Simplification Centre, London, gave a presentation at the International Conference on Contract Simplification at the Swiss Re Centre for Global Dialogue. Find out more here: http://cgd.swissre.com/events/contract_simplification.html#tab_1

"We did some work quite a long time ago now for a charity for older people about how to communicate with people in later years of life. We used simpler language and they were very worried that they would feel patronized, we're infantilizing them, treating them like children. They didn't say that, they said no, we like simple language, we like the large type because at least we can understand what you're trying to tell us.

Not everybody agrees what complex language is and what simple language is. It's very hard to imagine what it's like not to know what you know, in other words, to come to something from new. Also, your education, you're praised for using long words and complex language by your teachers. There's a conservatism that this language has always worked and why should we change it? There's risk it won't be as precise. There's also a great fear of dumbing down. Often we're accused of dumbing things down, that is, I'm disrespecting my reader. It might make them feel stupid because I'm using plain language, which is very rarely the case, of course.

Sometimes when you're working with clients you can be locked into, I wouldn't say a dispute but a point where you're not getting anywhere because they've got their words and they're confident in them and we're coming along and saying, you've been doing it wrong all these years. We're not even lawyers but actually there's so much going on, that it's, I think, really hopeful that things will change, because we're in a new digital age and things have to change, don't they?"

title: Michael Mainelli, on how the insurance industry has led the way in blockchain technology
description:
The Executive Chairman of Z/Yen Group talking at the International Conference on Contract Simplification at the Swiss Re Centre for Global Dialogue. Find out more here: http://cgd.swissre.com/events/contract_simplification.html#tab_1

"Block chain technology is an indelible, immutable database. It's persistent, pervasive, and permanent. It's sounds in fact like a bad database. It's very difficult to change it. What makes it exciting though, it allows people to work together in a way that the central third party doesn't form a natural monopoly and therefore it's ideally suited to financial services where multiple parties need to work together over long periods of time.
The principle benefits of block chain technology are very much that it is indelible and immutable. People cannot change records. They can only add to the records. This allows people to share records confidently knowing what the full audit trail is. Ledgers are boring, exceedingly boring but they underlie all the financial services and this is effectively a new type of ledger technology that people can use confidently.


I don't feel that the insurance industry has been slow to adopt block chain technology. On the contrary, some of our very first clients in this sector really came out of insurance. It was insurers who approached us to take some of our technology forward. In fact, we've launched our last month an insure on the block chain technology for the shared economy, Save Share, so in many ways the insurance industry is ahead of the banking and some of the trading sectors in the true adoption of this technology rather than behind."

title: Rory Unsworth, Swiss Re, on how simplified contracts can help close the protection gap
description:
The Head of the Contracts Centre, Swiss Re, gave a presentation at the International Conference on Contract Simplification at the Swiss Re Centre for Global Dialogue. Find out more here: http://cgd.swissre.com/events/contract_simplification.html#tab_1

"The opportunities for the insurance industry seem quite significant to me. Here I'm thinking particularly of the protection gap. The protection gap is caused, of course not only by the complexity of the product, the complexity of the contract that it's sold with, but that is a significant contributor to under insurance. I think the upside is enormous.
Contracts have become so complex out a misguided sense of self protection. People have the impression that if they use them as a wall of separation between them and their customer that they will be better protected. Actually the mindset is wrong there. It's really about contracts as an instrument for collaboration with our customers. The second piece I think is about laziness to a certain extent. That we have given it the resources it needs. We tend to throw everything into the contract and we leave it there. We don't spend the time that's needed to simplify.

To eliminate complexity from the process, the first piece is really to think about eliminating complexity, and to give it the resources it needs to take that seriously. After that it's really just a question of resources and the strategic approach.
The techniques are not yet that well known for simplification. Here at the conferences we've talking about those techniques and we've collected them. I think once you have them and you can change the mindset as well, you're equipped."

When you're diagnosed with a disease like this your life changes, you stop leading the sort of hectic life in which sometimes you can't even find time to sit down and enjoy a cup of coffee. You think, "What have I done with my life up to now? And what do I want to do now?". So from then on you savour every moment of your life. The fact is that medical treatment keeps me alive. I love to enjoy myself because one values the present."

title: Nick Szabo, Global Financial Assets, on bitcoin, blockchain and the benefits of smart contracts
description:
The CEO of Global Financial Assets Inc. spoke at the International Conference on Contract Simplification at the Swiss Re Centre for Global Dialogue. Find out more here: Find out more here: http://cgd.swissre.com/events/contract_simplification.html#tab_1

"Where smart contracts work best is well-defined financial contracts so smart contracts can greatly improve the cross border plumbing and the financial relationships that the insurance companies have with other financial companies and with other people in the world. In fact, to the extent they're dependent on companies, prime brokers, and big banks like JP Morgan and Chase and Goldman Sachs and so forth, they can reduce that dependency by talking more directly to their counter parties through smart contracts rather than through a prime broker.

For data protection, block chain is a very powerful tool for proving that something is not, it's like freezing something in amber, you can prove it hasn't been forged or altered after you put it on the block chain. Data confidentiality is much harder. There is a technology that is called zero knowledge computations that will allow for very small computations that are very important for the confidentiality, a very limited amount of confidential data processing but unfortunately, it doesn't scale so for big data, it's basically impossible to associate that with a block chain to keep it confidential.
Bitcoin works the same everywhere and Etherium. They work the same everywhere in Albania, Zimbabwe, Argentina, United States, China, everywhere all over the world they work the same way with the same set of rules. We're going to see this being done more widely in the financial community that instead of nationally siloed financial systems that are dependent on law enforcement we're going to see more global vending machine like financial transactions."

title: Roche - Doing now what patients need next
description:
"We believe it's urgent to deliver medical solutions right now -- even as we develop innovations for the future. We are passionate about transforming patients' lives. We are courageous in both decision and action. And we believe that good business means a better world.

That is why we come to work each day. We commit ourselves to scientific rigour, unassailable ethics, and access to medical innovations for all. We do this today to build a better tomorrow.

We are proud of who we are, what we do, and how we do it. We are many, working as one across functions, across companies, and across the world.
We are Roche."

title: Carol Savage, IBM, on how IBM simplified its Cloud Services Agreement
description:
The Corporate Legal Director, Global Client Contract Development, IBM, talking at the talking at the International Conference on Contract Simplification at the Swiss Re Centre for Global Dialogue. Find out more here: http://cgd.swissre.com/events/contract_simplification.html#tab_1

"The cloud services agreement was revolutionary because it was a departure and a change, a shift from how we traditionally wrote contracts. We moved from the traditional approach of writing with legalese, of ensuring that aspects of the contract were covered in maybe multiple parts to actually a very simplified approach only including those terms that were required, not those nice to haves or the luxuries and also really breaking it down into very plain English that would be easy for our clients to understand.
I'd say the challenges were centered around ensuring that our stakeholders were with us and that we had buy-in. I think having sponsorship from our senior leaders was helpful in that regard. Then the second challenge was actually coming up with a single simplified agreement that would accommodate a very robust and wide portfolio of cloud offerings.
A consumer wants to be able to buy anything from a small cents on a minute type of cloud offering all the way up to something that's far more robust and not have to look at multiple different types of contracts, different types of terms. If you can simplify it and include everything in one contract, that makes it far more easier for them to do business with you."

title: Freedom EVO® PCR workstation
description:
Looking for effortless touchscreen operation and step-by-step instructions to set up PCR reactions?

The TouchTools™ PCR Wizard offers straightforward automation of PCR reaction set-up on the Freedom EVO® PCR workstation, using the high precision air displacement pipetting arm. The Wizard simplifies reaction set-up for a wide range of applications -- from end-point, real-time and multiplex PCR protocols, to sequencing, genotyping and gene expression methods, as well as pathogen and mutagenesis detection -- helping to reduce training time and costs for research and diagnostic laboratories.

The PCR Wizard's intuitive TouchTools interface and step-by-step instructions guide users through the set­up process, allowing sample information to be entered manually, imported from a file or identified by barcode scanning. Users can quickly and easily optimize 96- and 384-well PCR plate layouts to meet specific workflow needs, allowing up to 96 Master Mixes and multiple PCR plates per run, with their own controls and dilution curves. The worktable layout for each experiment is visually displayed, outlining where all necessary reagents and labware should be placed, as well as the volume and quantity of each item required to complete the run, minimizing the risk of failed experiments. Seamless data transfer to Bio-Rad and Life Technologies real-time cyclers avoids transcription errors, and optional integrated sample tracking further increases process security.

title: Srdjan Capkun on diverse security cyber risk prevention
description:
Srdjan Capkun, Professor, Department of Computer Science, Institute of Information Security, ETH Zurich, gave a keynote on "Lessons learned from high profile data breaches" at the Expert Forum on Cyber Risk at the Swiss Re Centre for Global Dialogue. Find out more here: http://cgd.swissre.com/events/Expert_Forum_Cyber_Risk_2016.html#tab_2

Srdjan Capkun:
There are many lessons that we can learn from different breaches but what we can see is that the protection levels are still relatively low in a lot of today's systems. The barrier of entry for attacks is there for very low. The internet is a complex organism and it requires the protection of front systems like the ones of Target and other entities from breath. It also requires the protection of the network itself. We need to make sure that the data that flows through the network does not go through the jurisdictions or geographic areas that we don't want. We need to confine these flows either geographically or within certain jurisdictions and we have to change the trust model of the internet as well. In the current internet we trust to many entities when we go online and these are certificate authorities that are distributed globally in many jurisdictions and controlled by private entities as well. It's very hard to operate in this trust model. 

My advice is not to optimize for compliance but instead to try to build your systems to be secure. It is very easy to build a system to be compliant with regulations but still leave many vulnerabilities and by default this is what happens if one optimizes only for compliance. Diversity, if we diversify the security solutions we will then make the job of the attacker much more difficult because the attackers cannot then re-use their tools across different organizations.

title: Phil Kibler on adding value to cyber risk insurance
description:
Phil Kibler, Partner, Global Security Services Competency Leader, IBM, gave a talk on "The business risk from cyber and the increasing involvement of the CRO" at the Expert Forum on Cyber Risk at the Swiss Re Centre for Global Dialogue. Find out more here: http://cgd.swissre.com/events/Expert_Forum_Cyber_Risk_2016.html#tab_2

Phil Kibler: 
I think where data is located and what companies are doing with that data has become a real challenge for them. In past years, it was much easier. It was always on site. It was always controlled by them, and categorization of that data was easy. Now, it's in the cloud. Now, what data is even considered from a privacy standpoint? What do I have out in the public domain? What do I have in the private domain? Frankly has made that much more challenging for companies in trying to figure out exactly what is data and what is private and how to protect that data from being discovered.

I think with the proliferation of data and the types of data that people have; it's in the cloud, it's structured, it's unstructured within their own environment. I think those are the real challenges. I think companies today have a much tougher time knowing where all their data is, and being able to categorize their data to be able to meet the regulatory requirements that are present in today's world.

I'm actually surprised given the nature of the cyber risk that's out there that cyber insurance is not more on the uptake. I think the biggest challenge that insurance carriers face is a getting the knowledge out there of what that product actually does versus what people may perceive that it does, and I think the other challenge is making sure that they are perceived as "value add". Not something that is just a necessary thing that people do, but that it actually brings real value to the discussion of risk mitigation, and how they can be present in helping to mitigate cyber threats that companies face.

title: HP D300 Digital Dispenser delivers any dose to any well
description:
The HP D300 Digital Dispenser offers a simple method for streamlining dose response curve set up, offering picoliter to microliter non-contact dispensing of small molecules in DMSO directly into your assay plate. Fast and accurate determination of a candidate compound's IC50 provides drug discovery biologists with valuable information for the development of new pharmaceuticals, yet traditional techniques are both time¬ consuming and laborious, with no standardization across the industry.

Using HP's Direct Digital Dispensing technology, this convenient benchtop solution requires almost no set up time, and single use T8 Dispenseheads virtually eliminate the risk of cross¬ contamination. It allows rapid delivery of any dose to any well, saving time, minimizing waste of valuable compounds and accelerating drug discovery.

title: Anna Maria D'Hulster on potential protection gaps
description:
Anna Maria D'Hulster, the Secretary General and Managing Director of The Geneva Association, welcomed speakers and attendees to the 11th CRO Assembly: Technological and societal change, at the Swiss Re Centre for Global Dialogue. Find out more here: http://cgd.swissre.com/events/11th_CRO_Assembly_Technological_and_societal_change.html

She talks to Jennifer Davies from the Swiss Re CGD about the fast emerging risks and challenges for the insurance industry.

JD: What are the biggest challenges for the insurance industry as technologies and societies change?

Anna Maria D'Hulster: There are a lot of challenges, but there are also opportunities for us at the same time. I think the major one is the availability of data. That is totally changing. Traditionally, the insurance model was to collect data about as many policy holders as possible to pool the information on specific risks, and then underwrite risks and compensate for claims due to these risks and specific events. Now with the availability of data – and this availability is increasing – 90 percent of data has become available in the last two years; with the availability of data, the game is changing. We know a lot more. We can, together with processing capacity, and algorithms, provide a specific risk for one specific policy holder, in a specific situation, at a specific moment. We can create pricing which is kind of segment of one risk-based pricing. That's the opposite of what we used to do. Where used to have a collective view on risk, and now we have a single view on the risk. That's for us the major change that is happening.

JD: Where can the insurance industry find new opportunities?

Anna Maria D'Hulster: There are lots. If you look at the involvement of the GDP, and the involvement of internet use worldwide. Compare that to the increasing premium volume, and increase of assets and risks insured, you see that the gap between the global growth and growth of the insurance industry is increasing. There is a potential to get. We call that potential the "potential protection gap". For instance, we know that only 70 percent of claims coming out of catastrophies are insured. There's also a protection gap in the area of longevity, especially also health and when people get older, and there is a protection gap in the area that we are discussing today - emerging risks, like cyber-risks, etc. That is what we see. 

The new technologies, and a large amount of data should allow us to take a different approach and to look how we can, in a new way, or in another way cover these risks. Also, it may be possible for insurers to focus more on prevention and risk-management skills that we have always had. It's definitely, for us, an interesting discussion, and an interesting conference. We must embrace the change. This is a tool, and a way for us to do it.

JD: Anna Maria D'Hulster, thank you very much.

Anna Maria D'Hulster: Thank you.

title: sigma 6/2015 - Life insurance in the digital age
description: The life insurance industry is at a crossroads: new technologies pave the way for a revolution in the way insurers write business, handle data and engage consumers. Swiss Re's latest sigma study Life insurance in the digital age: fundamental transformation ahead looks at how life insurers have adopted some of the capabilities now available and how the future may take shape. It explores issues such as the use of Big Data, cognitive computing and how digital data can help insurers broaden their reach. The report also explores looming challenges for life insurers such as creating a positive digital experience for consumers, keeping up with regulatory changes, especially around data privacy, and dealing with potential partnerships or competition from non-traditional players.

title: Rasoul Jalali from Uber on the company's success
description:
Rasoul Jalali, the General Manager from Uber gave a one-to-one interview talk at the 11th CRO Assembly on the topic "Uber: the rise of real-time ride sharing". Find out more here: http://cgd.swissre.com/events/11th_CRO_Assembly_Technological_and_societal_change.html#tab_1

He talks to Jennifer Davies about the business.

JD: Why do you think Uber has become so successful so quickly? 

Rasoul Jalali : I think one of the major reasons is the simple use of our app. By one push of a button, you can request a ride, there's cashless payments, you can rate your drivers, and the whole magical experience around using an Uber. 

JD: Airbnb is another popular company in the sharing economy, are there any others to look out for?

Rasoul Jalali : I think there are lots of new companies that are popping up everyday, and certainly a lot of them are also very interesting. I think for me personally the ones that have a lot of chance right now are in the crowdfunding environment, which I believe is a very strong development.  

JD: Which industries are likely to be disrupted in the future? 

Rasoul Jalali : I think in general, industries that are highly protected, a lot of regulations around them, not using technology, these industries are most certainly prone to disruption in the future. If you build up walls around your industry, around what you are doing, these walls most certainly have to come down by the use of technology. We will see a couple of industries being shaken up in the future. 

JD: Can you name which ones? 

Rasoul Jalali: Thinking again about the finance industries, we see a lot of "fin' tech" companies coming up, having challenges in terms of regulation, but also winning here and there on the regulatory battlefield and being able to provide new and better solutions.

JD: Rasoul Jalali, thank you very much.

Rasoul Jalali: Thanks a lot.

title: Patrick Raaflaub on change in the insurance industry
description:
Patrick Raaflaub, the Group Chief Risk Officer from Swiss Re welcomed speakers and attendees to the 11th CRO Assembly: Technological and societal change, at the Swiss Re Centre for Global Dialogue. Find out more here: http://cgd.swissre.com/events/11th_CRO_Assembly_Technological_and_societal_change.html
He speaks to Jennifer Davies from Swiss Re CGD about the fast moving changes that will impact the insurance industry.

JD: What are the biggest challenges for the insurance industry as technologies and societies change?

Patrick Raaflaub: I think there will be many changes to the traditional way of providing insurance. A lot of the statistical underpinnings might be challenged, people will know more. Information asymmetries will evolve. So I think there will be many challenges. The question is also how quickly does society respond and adapt. How will it respond to the new possibilities that are being developed, that are being implemented in the technology space, not just in digital technology but also in the application of that technology to other spaces of technology.

JD: Where can the insurance industry find new opportunities?

Patrick Raaflaub: What is critically important is to really stay attuned to try to understand what is happening and be very adaptable. Probably it will be harder in the future to provide very long term insurance products. 

Products might become more shorter- term, more diverse also, and we will have to get accustomed to the pace of change, for it will become much more rapid and that will change a lot of our internal behavior.

JD: Can it move fast enough?

Patrick Raaflaub:  Well, it will have to, otherwise it will just become obsolete. But personally, I'm quite optimistic. I think the concept of our business is really the business of financing risk. You know we say we manage risk and to some extent we do, but what we actually do is we finance risk taking and I think that will become, if anything, even more relevant in the future. So, as long as we adapt our offering to these needs, we can stay very relevant.

JD: Patrick Raaflaub, thank you very much.
 
Patrick Raaflaub: Thank you.

title: Roche Voices: Sophie Kornowski about accessing external innovation
description:
At Roche we were one of the first companies to recognise the importance of external innovation. Since then, we have maintained a model that values external innovation as highly as our own internally derived discoveries and established collaborations as the cornerstone of our R&D strategy. Découvrez l'épisode d'Eurêka ! consacré à l’ulcère à l’estomac de Barry J. Marshall et Robin Warren
Novartis, héritier d’une tradition de recherche et de progrès médical et leader mondial dans l’offre de médicaments, s’associe avec Les Productions du Moment pour la création d’un nouveau programme court, intitulé Eurêka ! Cette série pédagogique et ludique met en lumière les grandes découvertes scientifiques et médicales qui ont marqué notre histoire.
Eurêka ! revient sur les histoires, souvent insolites et surprenantes, ayant mené les grands chercheurs à faire naître l’innovation, et leur apport à la médecine d’aujourd’hui. Les épisodes mettent en scène des personnages animés, mêlant dessins et objets réels sous le crayon du célèbre dessinateur Serge Bloch.

title: Eurêka ! : making of
description:
Découvrez le making of de la série Eurêka !

A travers la série, les Productions du Moment, Novartis et France Télévisions ont souhaité mettre en scène, de manière pédagogique et amusante, les histoires insolites et parfois surprenantes qui ont mené aux grandes découvertes médicales et scientifiques. Vu par le prisme de l’anecdote, Eurêka ! raconte à chaque épisode la petite histoire dans la grande et fait ainsi entrer le téléspectateur dans les coulisses de ce monde parfois inaccessible. La série dévoile ces grands inventeurs sous un nouveau jour. Parce qu’ils étaient amoureux, curieux, pudiques, maladroits ou déterminés, ces grands personnages de l’Histoire ont devancé leur temps, en donnant naissance à des découvertes qui ont parfois bouleversé le cours de la médecine et de la science.
Les épisodes  seront diffusés,  chaque jour durant 1 mois,  sur France 5 à 13h30  avant le Magazine de la Santé à compter du  16 novembre  2015.

title: Sika ComfortFloor®, a professional flooring solution for a perfect look
description: Sika ComfortFloor® is liquid applied resin flooring material and is available in bespoken colours. This modern material technology provides another new option for floor design with smooth and seamless surface which has no flaw and looks perfect. It can be applied for your floor at home, or floors in commercial and public buildings. Sika ComfortFloor® is more than just a sleek, stylish floor. It’s about how people see and respond to the cool look and the warm touch.  It is odorless, solvent free and its smooth polyurethane surface makes it anti-allergic as no dust or bacteria can stick to the surface. It’s also very easy to clean and maintain, and durable enough to withstand constant wear.

title: Infinite® 200 PRO with Gas Control Module (GCM™): Read & incubate cells in parallel
description:
This innovative Gas Control Module (GCM™) provides rigorous environmental control within the detection chamber, making the Infinite® 200 PRO ideal for cell-based applications. An integrated gas inlet allows external control of CO2 and O2 simultaneously helping to maintain stable culture conditions and improve cell growth. It also allows assays to be performed under anaerobic or physiological conditions, extending the experimental window while ensuring consistent and biologically relevant data.
The GCM together with its unique altitude correction and the integrated quality control of acoustic and visible warnings leads to more consistent and reliable data and makes it indispensable for cell-based applications.

title: Drawn to Science: The glycosylation of antibodies
description:
Antibodies, like many other natural proteins, are normally decorated with sugars. Optimizing the pattern of sugars associated with synthetic antibodies can have an enormous impact on their therapeutic efficacy. This technology, know as glycoengineering, is the focus of the Roche research center in Zurich, Switzerland. 
http://www.roche.com/research_and_development/what_we_are_working_on/research_technologies/protein-related_technologies/glycoengineering.htm

description:
Head of Swisstransplant Dr. Franz Immer and Roche General Counsel Dr. Gottlieb Keller talking about organ donation and transplantation. According to international health organisations, a new patient is added to the organ transplant waiting list every ten minutes.  More information on organ donation: http://www.swisstransplant.org

title: Sika CarboShear: Structural Strengthening for shear load - How to apply L-shaped CFRP profiles
description: A unique Sika solution for external shear strengthening of T-beams. The shear capacity of reinforced concrete-beams can be significantly increased by externally applied Sika CFRP strengthening systems. The ideal solution is the unique Sika CarboShear L-shaped CFRP profiles, which are bonded onto the beam and anchored into the top slab, with the Sikadur®-30 and -30 LP structural epoxy resin based adhesives – Simple, but highly effective and efficient shear strengthening.

title: Biotech Production at Roche in Basel
description:
Only a few decades ago the idea of harnessing biotechnology to produce medicines appealed highly fanciful. That is because unlike conventional drug ingredients which were produced by purely chemical methods, biotechnologically produced medicines are much more complex and difficult to manufacture. Today these drugs have become must haves to the treatment of several serious diseases. Biotechnologically produced treatment tools are now available in several disease areas, cancer in particular, representing a beacon of hope for thousands of patients. Roche is the world no 1 in biotechnology. With its Research Development and Production sites gathered across the globe, the Roche Group offers doctors and patients alike a vast range of biopharmaceuticals and diagnostic tests manufactured using biotechnology. One biotechnology production site is located at group headquarters in Basel and is presented in the video. http://www.roche.com

More videos on http://www.youtube.com/user/roche

title: Biotech Produktion bei Roche in Basel
description:
Noch vor wenigen Jahrzehnten schienen biotechnologisch produzierte Medikamente in weiter Ferne zu liegen. Im Gegensatz zu herkömmlichen, rein chemisch hergestellten Wirkstoffen sind Biotech-Medikamente viel komplexer und aufwändiger zu produzieren. Heute sind diese Produkte aus der Behandlung schwerwiegender Krankheiten nicht mehr wegzudenkenken. In vielen Indikationsgebieten, insbesondere Krebs, stehen heute Behandlungsmethoden auf biotechnologischer Basis zur Verfügung und geben Hoffnung für tausende von Patienten. Roche ist in der Biotechnologie weltweit führend. Mit Forschungs-, Entwicklungs- und Produktionsstandorten rund um die Welt liefert die Roche Gruppe ein breites Sortiment an Bio-Pharmazeutika und diagnostischen Tests auf der Basis biotechnischer Herstellung für Ärzte und Patienten. Eine der Biotech Produktions-Anlagen befindet sich in Basel und wird in diesem Video vorgestellt. http://www.roche.com

description: The number of people aged 65 years and above will grow by around 80% to nearly 1 billion over the next 15 years. Meeting the care needs of the elderly will be one of the main challenges facing society. Swiss Re's latest sigma study http://www.swissre.com/sigma/ "How will we care? Finding sustainable long-term care solutions for an ageing world" examines how tightening government budgets, changing demographics, low personal means and limited awareness need to be addressed if this challenge is to be met. You can explore 34 years of insurance market data here at http://sigma-explorer.com/.

title: HydroFlex™
description:
Learn more about the HydroFlex 3-in-1 microplate washer for automated washing of ELISA assays, magnetic beads and non-magnetic beads, as well as gentle cell washing of loosely adherent cells.

Tecan's HydroFlex 3-in-1 microplate washer provides you with increased productivity and sparkling results by accelerating key washing processes for a range of applications such as:

automated ELISA-washing with online process control 
gentle washing of adherent and loosely adherent cells 
gentle washing of magnetic beads used for multiplexed assays 
washing of small magnetic beads used for bead-based ELISA 
automated washing of non-magnetic beads via vacuum filtration 
automated DNA-purification after PCR-step via vacuum filtration 
gentle washing of protein arrays spotted on segmented slides 
or into 96-well microarray plates 
    
      
Reflecting over 25 year of expertise in advanced liquid handling at Tecan, the HydroFlex offers excellent performance, reliability and flexibility for a variety of plate types in 96-well format.

Its outstanding control systems set new standards for plate washing, with options including an online process control manifold that monitors all overflow-wash steps, and a liquid level detection system to monitor filling levels in wash buffer and waste bottles online.

Based on extensive testing the HydroFlex provides a unique proof of quality documented in an MTTF-record (mean time to first failure record) over more than 40,000 plates, typically corresponding to several years of successful operation.

The HydroFlex 3-in-1 plate washer is easily operated as a stand-alone instrument with its integrated keypad, or by using the intuitive HydroControl™ software.

It fulfills user administration requirements, including electronic records and signatures, and has been designed to meet the 21 CFR part 11 regulations. Additionally the HydroFlex for ELISA washing has been designed to meet the IVD 98/79/EC directive for Europe.

title: Sika AnchorFix® Solid Masonry / Concrete - solutions for all your anchoring needs
description:
Sika AnchorFix® chemical anchors are suited for low to ultra-high load applications, as the resulting bond is stronger than the base substrate material itself in almost all instances. 

Additionally, as the system is based on chemical adhesion, no pre-loading stress is imparted to the substrate, as it is with expansive mechanical anchors. 

Therefore, chemical anchors are also ideal for fixing close to edges; they can be installed at reduced centres and are best for close group anchoring. SikaAnchorFix® products are available for elementary and structural applications also considering the requirements of different climate zones.

title: sigma explorer: the insurance data you need at your fingertips
description: The new web-based tool from Swiss Re's Economic Research and Consulting team gives you access to sigma's key historical data whenever you want with sigma explorer. We all see images of shocking devastation and listen to stories of human tragedy when a disaster happens. What we don't always get, in hindsight, is appreciation of the total losses of a disaster. The news agenda moves on well before the full toll is known. Have you ever wondered, for example, what the total losses - human and economic - of the 2011 earthquake and tsunami in Japan were?  Sigma explorer helps you find out how many lives were lost, how much infrastructure was destroyed, and what the overall financial cost was. The sigma explorer is an interactive, web-based app that gives you access to information about catastrophes and world insurance premiums from as far back as 1970 from almost any internet device. You can use sigma explorer  to visualize and share key historical data about the number of lives lost or the level of insured and uninsured losses overall and for specific events. "The sigma explorer is unique tool for anyone with an interest in the insurance risk business," says Swiss Re Chief Economist Kurt Karl. "Nowhere else is so much industry related information readily, and graphically, available for all."

title: TM 160 - 250 - 315 ECO S: Butt fusion machines for construction site - GF Piping Systems
description:
Hydraulically operated butt fusion machines with manual control unit to joint PE, PP, PB pipes and fittings for pressure piping systems on building sites and in trenches, up to diameter 315 mm.

TM ECO S is designed for installers who need a machine with a simple configuration: the built-in thermometer and heater controller, in combination with the optimized weight of other machine components, makes the operation easier and faster.

The mechanical features of the TM ECO S ensure a proper performance also in harsh environments.

TM ECO is compatible with the WR 200 S welding recorder (available as accessory) to assist the welder and monitor the complete fusion sequence. 

For more information visit our website:

title: Gennaro Aliperti's hopes for a human future
description:
For Swiss Re's Open Minds project we capture a global diversity of voices. What do people from around the world think about longevity, food security, climate change, natural disasters and sustainable energy solutions? 

Gennaro "Jerry" Aliperti run's Emily's Pork Store in Williamsburg, Brooklyn. In this clip he shares his thoughts and concerns about technology and remembers the days when people weren't busy texting, but playing ball outside instead. Is our interconnected world destroying traditional communities? As Jerry says "I wish technology would slow down a bit."

Watch the clip, then visit us on the Open Minds platform: http://openminds.swissre.com/
Tell us what's on your mind, find out what others discuss and join the conversations.

Read more about Gennaro Aliperti here: http://newyork.seriouseats.com/2012/06/emilys-pork-store-east-williamsburg-italian-brooklyn-charcuterie-salami-market-tour.html

description:
De MSA 4.0 is de eerste van een nieuw tijdperk elektrolasmachines: sneller, efficiënter en betrouwbaarder dan ooit tervoren. Deze “lichtgewicht” machine is zeer gebruiksvriendelijk en geschikt voor elektrolasverbindingen van d16 - d1400 mm. Interessante extraatjes zijn de geïntegreerde GPS en de slimme scanner waarmee u barcodes én QR-codes kunt inlezen. Bovendien kunt u met de bijhorende software op een eenvoudige manier uw lassen opvolgen, zonder bergen papierwerk te moeten verzetten! Met de handige USB- stick kunt u tevens de lasprotocols bijhouden. U bent benieuwd naar de nieuwe MSA 4.0 machine? Bekijk onze video en “Like”!

Voor België en Luxemburg: Neem contact op met ons MSA Service Center in Brussel voor meer informatie.

=======
The new MSA 4.0 marks a new era for electrofusion solutions: faster, more efficient and reliable than ever before. This lightweight machine is very easy to use and perfect for electrofusion joints ranging from d16 up to d1400 mm. Advanced features include an integrated GPS and a smart scanner that can read both barcodes and QR codes. The software allows you to keep track of all joints, without too much paper work on the side! Welding protocols can be transferred to an USB-stick. Interested to know more about the MSA 4.0 welding machine? 
Watch our video and “Like”!

title: Vorschau: Swissbau Focus Arena - Wertsteigerung durch Gebäudelabels
description:
LIVE am Mittwoch, 18. Januar 2012 um 16 Uhr in der Swissbau Focus Arena (Halle 1.2), an der Swissbau in Basel!

Im Prozess der Erneuerung des Schweizer Gebäudeparks spielen Gebäudelabels eine entscheidende Rolle. Labels helfen dabei, ein Gebäude im Hinblick auf bestimmte Kriterien zu bewerten. Während Minergie die «energetische Güte» eines Gebäudes zertifiziert, berücksichtigen andere Labels umfassende Leitbilder nachhaltigen Bauens. Solche Labels, wie LEED aus den USA, BREEAM aus England und das DGNB aus Deutschland drängen immer stärker auf den Schweizer Markt. 

In der Swissbau Focus Arena vom Mittwoch, 18. Januar 2012, wird diese Entwicklung zur Debatte gestellt. Wird nachhaltiges Bauen messbar? Welche Standards brauchen wir? Wie setzen wir diese um? Und wie verändert sich die Immobilienwirtschaft dadurch?

Moderiert wird die Arena von Catherine Mettler. Die Diskussionsteilnehmer im inneren Kreis sind:
Roger Baumann, Head Business Development & Sustainability, Credit Suisse
Franz Beyeler, Geschäftsführer Minergie
Alec von Graffenried, Nationalrat und Direktor für nachhaltige Entwicklung, Losinger Marazzi AG
Markus Koschenz, Geschäftsführer Reuss Engineering AG, Gisikon

Zur Veranstaltungsteilnahme treffen Sie bitte pünktlich um 15.30 Uhr bei der Arena im Swissbau Focus (Halle 1.2) ein. Die Veranstaltung beginnt um 16.00 Uhr und wird live im Regionalfernsehen übertragen. Bitte beachten Sie, dass wir Ihnen auch mit Anmeldung keinen Sitzplatz garantieren können. Bei grossem Andrang kann die Swissbau Focus Arena vor Ort auf Screens mitverfolgt werden.

title: Sika® ViscoCim - More economical but achieves the impossible
description:
Reflecting the words of a customer:

"Sika® ViscoCim is applied like butter and gives for a flawless finish on the concrete surface treated".

The smooth rendering paste Sika® ViscoCim is a cosmetic product which covers up imperfections on concrete such as pinholes, gravel nets or honeycombs prior to painting. 

At the same time it is versatile as it can be mixed either with cement or with gypsum. 

It is an easy and fast application that blends with more cement or gypsum than other products. Therefore it is very cost-effective.

Sika® ViscoCim is ideal for craftsmen and plasterers working in Interior Finishing and Refurbishment.

"Introducing a new generation of Nescafe Alegria Coffee Solutions. Flexible, reliable and convenient, the Alegria offers you great coffee, consistently.
نقدم لكم جيل جديد من حلول قهوة نسكافيه ألجيريا . حيث أنها مرنة، وموثوقة، وسهلة التحضير، نسكافيه ألجيريا تقدم لكم قهوة لا مثيل لها.Designed as a result of over 85,000 engineering hours and with the involvement of over 60 industry experts, the new Alegria machines deliver greater flexibility, enhanced in-cup quality, improved operational efficiency and unbeatable simplicity
تعتبر نسكافيه ألجيريا نتيجة عمل أكثر من 85،000 ساعة هندسية وبمشاركة أكثر من 60 خبيراً في الصناعة، إن آلات ألجيريا الجديدة قادرة على تحقيق قدر أكبر من المرونة، والسهولة، وتعزيز الجودة وتحسين الكفاءة التشغيلية، إنها ببسا لا تقبل المنافسة
The Alegria is now available in three different sizes and options, and lets you choose from a selection of Espresso, Americano (black coffee), Café Latte, Cappuccino, Caramel Cappuccino, French Vanilla Latte, hot chocolate, and Cardamom tea.
تتوفر ألجيريا بثلاثة أحجام وخيارات مختلفة، حيث تتيح لك الاختيار من بين مجموعة اسبرسو، وأمريكانو (القهوة السوداء)، وقهوة لاتيه، وكابتشينو، وكابتشينو بالكاراميل، وقهوة لاتيه الفرنسية بالفانيليا، والشوكولاته الساخنة، والشاي الهيل."

title: Georg Fischer JRG AG: JRG CleanLine
description:
Schwebestoffe wie Kalk, Rost oder Sand können bei Reparaturen oder Sanierungen am öffentlichen Versorgungsnetzt über die Gebäudeanschlussleitung ins Hauswasser-Leitungsnetz gelangen. Die Wasserfilterung durch den JRG CleanLine verhindert die Verunreinigung des Leitungssystems durch Schwebestoffe. Die innovative Rückspültechnik ist äußerst wirkungsvoll. Dabei wird auf einen Bypassfilter verzichtet. Gleichzeitig überzeugt der Filter durch minimale Bauhöhen und lässt sich dank gleicher Baulängen wie die bewährten Druckreduzierventile JRGURED 1300/1130 und JRGURED Combi 1350 austauschen.

Für weitere Informationen besuchen Sie unsere Webseite: http://www.gfps.com/

title: SGKB Spot: Meine erste Bank
description:
Kaum etwas berührt uns Menschen mehr als das, was wir zum ersten Mal tun oder erleben: "Meine erste Liebe", "Mein erster Kuss", "Meine erste Wohnung", "Mein erstes Diplom", "Mein erstes Auto", "Mein erstes Kind". "Das erste Mal" bleibt für uns immer etwas ganz Besonderes.

Wir verbinden damit eine emotionale Wärme und Nähe, an die wir uns gerne erinnern. Und weil die St.Galler Kantonalbank in der Region so verwurzelt ist wie keine andere Bank, ist sie die richtige Partnerin, um Sie bei jedem "Ersten Mal" zu begleiten.

title: Dezentrale Lagerverwaltung - GF Piping Systems
description:
Das dezentrale Lagerverwaltungssystem von Georg Fischer Piping Systems wird nach Ihren Bedürfnissen individuell bestückt und direkt an Ihre Baustelle geliefert.

Der Arbeiter scannt den Artikelcode des benötigten Produkts ab, gibt die Stückzahl ein und kann das Produkt sofort installieren.

Durch die GF Verkaufsgesellschaften erfolgt die automatische Nachbeschickung des mobilen Warenlagers von Produkten mit Mindestmengenstatus, dadurch ist die kontinuierliche Fortführung Ihrer Baumassnahme gewährleitet.

Das dezentrale Lagerverwaltungssystem von Georg Fischer Piping Systems bietet Ihnen maximale Flexibilität und Verfügbarkeit auf der Baustelle.

title: Georg Fischer JRG AG: LegioTherm System
description: Das JRG LegioTherm-System hemmt und bekämpft das Wachstum von Biofilmen. Dies erfolgt mit einer Heisswasserspülung (thermischen Desinfektion) im Warmwasserzirkulationssystem. Krankheitsübertragende pathogene Keime wie z.B. Legionellen oder Pseudomonaden verbinden sich mit dem Biofilm und schützen sich so vor äusseren Einflüssen. Durch die thermische Desinfektion werden die Keime abgetötet.

title: GF 1600 Butt-Fusion Machine - GF Piping Systems - English
description:
The GF 1600 is a butt fusion machine for applications with pressure piping systems on building sites,suitable for jointing PE and PP pipes and fittings in the diameter range 1000 – 1600 mm. It has been designed to ensure quality and reliability of the welding process for water as well as gas applications, even in hard conditions (e.g. mining projects). Dedicated accessories are available to provide proper solutions for the various demands on construction site.

Highly precise and sturdy machine, provided with compact hydraulic unit and electric power box for safe connection of all electric devices. Especially designed to work according to the different national guidelines with low pressure cycles (DVS 2207, ISO 21307, WIS 4-32-08, NBN T 42-010, NEN 7200,...). Fully compatible with welding data logger WR 200 to guide the user through all preparation and fusion steps, ensuring the quality of the butt-fusion jointing.


For more information visit our website: 
http://www.gfps.com/appgate/ecat/common_flow/100005/COM/en/110019/110028/index.html

title: People and Productivity - Infrastructure
description:
People and Productivity - Insights by Nestlé UK.

Nestlé UK is proud to be part of the productivity success story in the food and drink industry.

Any modern manufacturing site is highly dependent on quality infrastructure to operate at maximum efficiency and capacity. 

Raw materials need to arrive at the site efficiently and finished product must be moved to market quickly. 

People need to arrive for shift-based work on time and be able to travel home again easily. No amount of internal efficiency can compensate for raw inputs or operators failing to arrive on time. 

As manufacturing operations become more productive through ‘just-in-time’ processes, reliable energy and transport networks for people and goods become ever more important.

Quality infrastructure is also among the most important dimensions of the inward investment case for the UK. A large port network with global connections and good onward links to the European continental market all make the UK attractive. Other aspects of infrastructure investment can contribute materially to inward investment business cases, for example protecting sites from flooding or weather damage, or improving sustainability performance through on-site energy generation.

Moving goods around the UK by road and rail is a constantly evolving challenge, requiring consistent investment. The World Economic Forum ranks the UK 27th globally for infrastructure quality. 

This baseline can be further eroded by disruptions in international supply chains, for example the Channel Tunnel closures in 2015.
Geographically distributed manufacturing sites across the North of England mean reliable transport for people is crucial for workforce planning, and consequently for productivity. 

Nestlé sites such as Tutbury are fortunate to have a large and able workforce on their doorstep, while others draw in people from further afield. The more efficient transport networks are, the wider this pool of employees, and the greater their flexibility in choosing where to live.

Ambitions for a ‘Northern Powerhouse’, built around integrated travel-to-work areas, could potentially play an important role in rebalancing growth across the UK. 

The concept is dependent on quality infrastructure, and should be a key factor in prioritising stretched transport budgets.

Find out more by visiting:

- http://www.nestle.co.uk/productivity

title: Understanding multiple sclerosis
description:
Multiple sclerosis (MS) is a disease of the central nervous system, estimated to affect 2.3 million people worldwide. It is a chronic disease in which the immune system abnormally attacks the insulation and support around the nerve cells (myelin sheath) in the brain, spinal cord and optic nerves, causing inflammation and consequent damage. MS is a leading cause of non-traumatic disability in young people, usually striking between 20 and 40 years of age. There is no cure for MS, but research continues to better understand and treat the disease. More about MS: http://www.roche.com/ms

title: People and Productivity - The food and drink sector
description:
People and Productivity - Insights by Nestlé UK.

Nestlé UK is proud to be part of the productivity success story in the food and drink industry.

The food and drink sector employs 380,000 people in the UK. It is at the core of adding value in the UK food chain, where its innovative new products and marketing expertise drive output in the important agriculture and retail sectors.

Productivity performance of food and drink since 2009 is impressive - an 11% increase in five years compares with a 0.6% increase in the economy as a whole. 

Food and drink faces important challenges.
Many in the workforce are approaching retirement age and more than 100,000 jobs will need to be filled by 2022. The sector is committed to apprenticeships and aims to treble its apprentice numbers by 2020. While the sector as a whole is expected to shrink slightly in employment terms, skilled employment is projected to rise. The sector pledged a 20% increase in the number of higher level, technical apprenticeships by 2017, ensuring that new workers match or exceed the productivity of those they replace.
There will remain a shortage of people with the crucial science, mathematics and engineering backgrounds: over 40% of managers in the sector report a struggle to fill highly skilled roles. Alongside other high value engineering sectors, food and drink is promoting these subjects in schools and colleges across the country and the rewarding careers they can lead to.

Find out more by visiting:

title: People and Productivity - Winning investment to the UK
description:
People and Productivity - Insights by Nestlé UK.

Nestlé UK is proud to be part of the productivity success story in the food and drink industry.

Capital investment for new, increased or upgraded production capacity is central to boosting productivity. 

Nestlé’s experience across the globe confirms that embracing new technology can deliver significant increases in output. 

The businesses with the best insight into those strengths are often the ones that are already here. According to UKTI, 740 of the 1,988 new investments in 2014 were expansions by existing investors. The track record that Nestlé’s British operations have built up over many decades is a crucial part of their case for fresh investment, and the most important factor in deciding where Nestlé locates investment globally. This type of investment is fiercely contested between managers inside multinational firms like Nestlé, who must champion their local operations. So much modern manufacturing depends on reliable delivery, that a clear, stable and compelling track record is often decisive in winning investment.
Understanding the ‘compound investment case’ for existing inward investors can help turn a history of inward investment and strong results into a future of strong productivity performance. Decisions to produce the Butterfinger product for the US market and Nescafé Dolce Gusto in the UK were direct consequences of outperforming other international locations over long periods of time, showing a track record of production and productivity, a strong base of skilled workers and support from local government and the community. In this sense, investment is won many years before it is ultimately made.

Find out more by visiting:
- http://www.nestle.co.uk/productivity/...
- http://www.nestle.co.uk/productivity
- https://twitter.com/nestleuknews #ukproductivity

title: People and Productivity - People and technology
description:
People and Productivity - Insights by Nestlé UK.

Nestlé UK is proud to be part of the productivity success story in the food and drink industry.

Combining investment in technology and investment in people is key to determining the productivity of individual firms. 

Nestlé’s experience shows how important it is to consider these two forms of investment as closely linked – and in many respects highly interdependent.

In most advanced manufacturing there is no such thing as automation: at Nestlé’s UK sites skilled people become more important to productivity performance as higher value machinery is introduced. 

estlé’s successful creation of a Coffee Centre of Excellence in Tutbury depended upon integrating new advanced machinery investments with ambitious workforce skills development. Getting the best out of next generation Nescafé Dolce Gusto machines, each producing 600 multi-component coffee pods per minute, started with shop floor operators in advance of installation.
Contrary to misperceptions about automation, this investment increased rather than reduced Nestlé’s need for highly skilled manual workers. This has been further proven with the building of a new freeze-dried coffee plant on site, with frontline operators participating in early procurement decisions, exposing them to the technology at overseas suppliers and other Nestlé sites. Operators and engineers systematically share knowledge with their peers, provide feedback and recommend improvements for further procurement and capacity expansions.
New operators start with a robust onboarding programme to ensure safety and quality compliance, followed by mentoring by experienced colleagues for 3-12 months depending on the complexity of the machinery. On top of job specific training in filling, packing and production, every year Nestlé invests over £1 million at Tutbury in 40-60 hours of continuous development per person. On-site coffee technologists, trained through a global network of coffee experts, develop robust training materials to enhance product mastership and provide education and assessment. In-house training experts support long-term technical and engineering skills acquisition through on-site programmes and NVQ accreditation.

Find out by visiting:
- http://www.nestle.co.uk/productivity/...
- http://www.nestle.co.uk/productivity
- https://twitter.com/nestleuknews #ukproductivity

title: People and Productivity - The power of bottom-up improvement
description:
People and Productivity - Insights by Nestlé UK.

Nestlé UK is proud to be part of the productivity success story in the food and drink industry.

Management and operational organisation are key differentiators of company performance, and important determinants of productivity. 

This is true across all sectors, but particularly in branded manufacturing where completely consistent high quality is critical. 

Like other advanced manufacturers, Nestlé’s model is built on understanding that the individual is key to effectiveness and efficiency.

Far from requiring top-down direction, Nestlé’s management principles emphasise building confidence among employees to take initiative. 

This means equipping them with technical understanding of the machines they use and giving them the skills to make improvements to processes, address problems and prevent them before they develop.

 The approach requires managers to step back and trust and empower their people, and the workforce to step up to the responsibility for their own efficiency. In Nestlé’s case the UK has been a marked success in this respect and there are more gains to be made.
This way of working is encapsulated in the Nestlé Continuous Excellence (NCE) concept and the disciplines of Total Productive Maintenance (TPM) that it incorporates. These provide the framework for ambitious structured programmes of coaching, feedback and teamwork at all levels. Both focus on finding local, bottom-up improvements to individual production lines.

The Aasted machine in Nestlé’s Halifax plant is a key component of its large production line producing Quality Street chocolates. For many years it operated at suboptimal efficiency, producing almost 5% wastage and requiring over 560 person hours to clean every week. It broke down once every 8 hours and was subject to a minor stoppage once every 82 minutes. 

In 2009 the Aasted machine and its operating team were inducted into a Total Productive Maintenance pilot.

Find out more by visiting:
- http://www.nestle.co.uk/productivity/...

title: What are antibody-drug conjugates and how do they work against cancer?
description:
A challenge of cancer treatment is doing our best to ensure that the benefits a medicine brings outweigh the burden of unwanted side effects. Chemotherapy, for example, is a powerful treatment that kills cancer cells, but it damages healthy cells too, leading to side effects like hair loss and tiredness. Monoclonal antibodies are also used to treat cancer and are capable of a more targeted attack on tumour cells.

What if we could combine the targeting power of monoclonal antibodies and the toxic properties of chemotherapy to help the treatment to selectively kill cancer cells? That’s where antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) come in.

title: What is pathological complete response (pCR) and why is it important?
description:
To determine whether a cancer medicine works in clinical trials, scientists typically use measurements called endpoints. In patients with early breast cancer, measuring an improvement in traditional endpoints such as disease-free survival can take many years, so it can take a long time to approve a medicine and get it to patients. This is a problem as almost a third of patients with early breast cancer will go on to develop advanced disease, at which point it becomes incurable.
A new endpoint called pathological complete response (pCR) may speed up approval of new medicines for patients with early breast cancer. Dr Andreas Chlistalla, Disease Area Head, Breast & Gynaecologic Malignancies, Global Medical Affairs at Roche explains what pCR is and why it is important. http://www.roche.com/clinical_trials.htm

title: ELGEF Plus Electrofusion Couplers - Operating Principle - GF Piping Systems - Swedish
description:
Constructing large dimension pipelines is challenging and requires specific designs of the pipeline components. The GF Piping Systems electrofusion couplers for water and gas applications are therefore made for a quick and reliable connection of your pipes and spigot fittings. Even under difficult site conditions the electrofusion couplers of GF Piping Systems convince with a long lasting and safe connection thanks to the active reinforcement.

A unique operating principle

Active reinforcement is a unique operating principle. The
functioning has its origin from the manufacturing technology where the outer pipe section is pressed over the inner pipe section.
During the fusion process, the active reinforcement fully effects: The heat in the fusion zone reduces the stiffness of the inner ring and the reinforcement ring pushes actively from the outside to the inner ring. Thus, larger gaps can be closed more quickly and the expansion of the coupler is inhibited. During the cooling process the constant pressure from the outside makes the coupler shrink onto the pipe. This patented principle ensures a perfect welding result.

For more information visit our website: http://www.gfps.com/appgate/ecat/common_flow/100005/COM/en/index.html

title: Apprentis retraités – Un coursier à vélo (22 ans) rencontre un sportif de haut niveau (96 ans)
description:

Réalisation: Barbara Kulcsár. Scénariste et réalisatrice, elle a étudié à Budapest, Barcelone et Zurich. Son plus grand succès jusqu'à présent est le film Nebelgrind.

title: How do we make decisions? | Herbert A. Simon | UBS Nobel Perspectives
description:
Herbert A. Simon’s work spanned a number of disciplines, from economics, to computer science, to cognitive psychology. He was curious how people solve problems and make decisions, especially within complex organizations. Simon described his research into artificial intelligence in the 1950s as the most important thing he did in his life, and the ripple effect of that work continues to be felt today.

Discover more about Herbert A. Simon https://www.ubs.com/herbert-simon
and explore the Nobel Perspectives series
https://www.ubs.com/nobel

title: Humanizing Artificial Intelligence - Professor Hiroshi Ishiguro's life work
description: Ishiguro, a professor at Osaka University’s Intelligent Robotics Laboratory, who made headlines with an android he built in his own likeness, considers that desire and intention are key prerequisites to achieving human AI. By 2020, he and his team hope to apply these emotions successfully in Erica, the most advanced humanoid yet – a collaborative effort between Osaka and Kyoto universities and the Advanced Telecommunications Research Institute International (ATR).

title: GF JRG Sanipex MT hydraulic bending - GF Piping Systems
description:
JRG Sanipex MT

The installation system JRG Sanipex MT consists of cross-linked PE-X or ML pipes and fittings which are made in a special two-layer injection technology, manufactured as fitting-in-fitting. High corrosion resistance characterizes the innovative and versatile system, which can be used in many different installation applications. The pipe connection is free of water pockets and provides the full flow rate without reduction of the cross-section. JRG Sanipex MT fittings are detachable and reusable, which brings enormous benefits when existing installations have to be extended. Thanks to simple and handy tools, installation times are significantly reduced.

title: The Art Market (in Four Parts): Art Fairs
description:
The Art Market, Explained: The Rise of the Art Fair

In 2015, art fairs generated an estimated $12.7 billion in profits for exhibiting galleries. But why do collectors attend fairs in droves? And what’s behind their rapid international proliferation? The fourth installment of “The Art Market (in Four Parts)” tracks how the art fair has transformed from a trade show into a platform where all aspects of the art market—galleries, collectors, curators, and artists—converge, and why they keep coming back. Fair directors and art-world influencers like Noah Horowitz, Matthew Slotover, Elmgreen & Dragset, Michele Maccarone, Josh Baer, and Sarah Thornton provide their insights.

Art Fairs is the final installment of a four-part documentary series, preceded by Auctions, Galleries, and Patrons. Together, the four segments tell a comprehensive story about the art market’s history and cultural influence.

This series is directed by Oscar Boyson and produced in collaboration with Artsy.
